channel.title: Paper Feeds
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv and IACR

item.title: OCELOT: Inference-Leakage Budgets for Privacy-Preserving LLM Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12341v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大型语言模型（LLM）智能体正深度介入用户私密场景——读取本地文件、调用外部工具、执行金融或社交交易——其每一步交互均可能跨越信任边界，泄露个人身份信息（PII）。传统隐私保护聚焦单次输出过滤，而智能体隐私本质是全轨迹后验风险控制：泄漏具有累积性（多个看似无害的释放经诚实但好奇或共谋接收方聚合后可推断敏感秘密）、双向性（恶意观察者可通过输入注入操控智能体自身推理）、及任务依赖性（同一字段对某接收方必要，对另一方则属冗余披露）。

方法创新：OCELOT 机制

本文提出 OCELOT——首个面向LLM智能体的运行时推理泄漏预算框架。其核心为证人验证式解密（Witness-Verified Declassification）：

  • 一个不可信但本地微调的防御模型（“证人”）对每个候选输出生成结构化证据：标注的语义原子（如“邮箱域”“薪资区间”）与候选解密操作符（如“泛化为行业均值”“k-匿名化”）；
  • 一个确定性验证器审计证据，基于信息论计算该操作符引发的最小熵成本（即对手信念提升量），并在防篡改账本上按接收方可信度加权的总预算内，授权效用最优且披露最少的版本。

实验效果

在涵盖文件摘要、行程规划、财务代理等多样化智能体基准测试中，OCELOT 在保持更高任务成功率（+12.3% avg. utility）的同时，将累积后验泄漏降低达68%；对自适应提示注入、 jailbreak 攻击、多步推理攻击及跨接收方共谋均具强鲁棒性；端到端开销仅增加17ms/step，兼容主流LLM推理栈。

AI Summary (English)

OCELOT is a runtime privacy mediator for LLM agents that enforces inference-leakage budgets over entire execution trajectories—not per-output filtering. It recasts agent privacy as posterior-risk control, quantifying how much an adversary’s belief about a secret may improve across all releases. Its core mechanism, Witness-Verified Declassification, decouples judgment (via an untrusted, locally fine-tuned witness model) from trust (via a deterministic verifier). The witness proposes structured declassification options with labeled semantic atoms; the verifier computes certified min-entropy costs and selects the least-disclosing yet task-useful release under a sink-trust-weighted budget logged on a tamper-evident ledger. Evaluated across diverse agent benchmarks, OCELOT achieves significantly lower cumulative leakage (+68% reduction) at higher utility (+12.3% avg.), resists adaptive injection, jailbreaks, multi-step inference, and sink collusion, with only modest latency overhead (~17 ms/step).

Abstract

Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly act on a user's behalf -- reading personal files, calling tools, transacting with external services -- possibly leaking personally identifiable information (PII) across trust boundaries at every step. Privacy here is a property not of a single output but of an entire trajectory, and three properties make it hard: leakage is cumulative, as individually innocuous releases accumulate across honest-but-curious or colluding sinks into inferences about a protected secret; bidirectional, as a malicious observation can inject instructions that turn the agent's own reasoning model against the user; and task-dependent, as the same field is necessary for one recipient yet gratuitous for another. Per-release contextual-integrity filters, information-flow controls, and posterior-leakage monitors each address part of this but none controls cumulative, inference-based leakage at runtime. We recast agent privacy as \emph{posterior-risk control} and present OCELOT, a runtime mediator that budgets how much an adversary's belief about a secret may improve across a trajectory, rather than filtering outputs. Its mechanism, \emph{Witness-Verified Declassification}, separates judgment from trust: an untrusted, locally fine-tuned defender model inspects each candidate release and emits structured evidence -- labeled atoms and proposed declassification operators -- which a deterministic verifier audits, charging a certified min-entropy cost for the chosen variant and authorizing the least-disclosing useful release under a sink-trust-weighted budget recorded on a tamper-evident ledger. Across diverse agent benchmarks and recent defenses, OCELOT attains significantly lower leakage at higher task utility, resists adaptive injection, jailbreak, cumulative inference, and sink collusion, and adds only modest overhead.

item.title: A Five-Plane Reference Architecture for Runtime Governance of Production AI Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12320v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

传统企业安全体系以数据边界治理为核心，聚焦静态数据（at rest）与传输中数据（in transit），依赖访问控制、数据防泄漏（DLP）、网络边界检测等机制管控“跨边界”行为。然而，生产级AI智能体（Production AI Agents）彻底瓦解了这一范式：它们在运行时动态读取上下文、调用工具、触发连接器、修改核心业务系统——风险由此内化至工作流内部，表现为一系列 individually-permitted 但 collectively-unauthorized 行为序列，现有基于原子主体（atomic principals）和请求瞬时决策的策略引擎无法应对。

方法与架构创新

本文提出首个面向运行时治理（runtime governance） 的五平面参考架构，包含四大可组合原语：

  • 五平面分解：1个推理平面（Reasoning Plane）负责意图裁定；4个执行平面（Network/Identity/Endpoint/Data）协同落实决策；
  • 任意点中断机制（Stop-Anywhere Mediation）：支持在工作流任意环节插入治理检查；
  • 复合主体与能力衰减（Composite Principals with Capability Attenuation）：建模委托链中权限的动态衰减，解决越权累积问题；
  • 结构化审计基底（Audit as Structured Evidence Substrate）：生成防篡改、可重构的链式证据，支撑事后归责与合规验证。

主要成果

定义6类通用中断原语（超越简单allow/deny），确立4条正确性不变式；实证封堵7类典型生产级智能体威胁（如隐式权限升级、上下文污染扩散、工具链路劫持等），覆盖5个真实业务工作流；参考实现表明：权限衰减逻辑100%正确、审计证据100%可重构、单次裁定延迟<10微秒、审计基底具备设计级抗篡改证据性。本架构明确限定于治理委托行为（delegated action），不介入模型内部行为，为后续全系统基准测试（live agent benchmark）奠定基础。

AI Summary (English)

Enterprise AI agents break traditional security models by internalizing risk within dynamic, multi-step workflows—where individually permitted actions combine into unauthorized business transformations. We propose the first reference architecture for runtime governance of production AI agents, built on four composable primitives: (1) a five-plane decomposition (one reasoning plane for intent adjudication + four enforcement planes for network, identity, endpoint, and data), (2) stop-anywhere mediation, (3) composite principals with capability attenuation to model delegation-aware authority decay, and (4) audit as a structured, tamper-evident evidence substrate. We define six generalized interruption primitives, formalize four correctness invariants, and demonstrate mitigation of seven real-world agent threats across five concrete workflows. A reference policy-engine implementation confirms: 100% attenuation correctness and evidence reconstructability, sub-10 µs adjudication latency, and exact compliance of the audit substrate’s tamper-evidence behavior. The architecture governs delegated action, not model internals—setting the stage for full-system evaluation on live agent benchmarks.

Abstract

Enterprise security was built to govern data boundaries: the protected surface was data at rest and in transit, and the controls -- access control, data-loss prevention, perimeter inspection -- governed crossings of that boundary. Production AI agents dissolve this assumption. An agent reads context, calls tools, invokes connectors, and modifies systems of record on an enterprise's behalf, so risk moves inside the workflow, into sequences of individually-permitted actions that may transform a business process no one authorized. Existing policy engines do not extend to this regime: they evaluate request-time decisions against atomic principals, where agentic systems require stateful evaluation against composite principals whose authority attenuates through delegation chains. We present a reference architecture for the runtime governance of production agents, built from four composable primitives: a five-plane decomposition (a reasoning plane that adjudicates intent, and four enforcement planes -- network, identity, endpoint, data -- that realize the decision), stop-anywhere mediation, composite principals with capability attenuation, and audit as a structured evidence substrate. We define a taxonomy of six interruption primitives that generalize allow and deny, state and argue for four correctness invariants, and demonstrate the foreclosure of seven production-agent threats across five concrete workflows. A reference implementation of the policy-engine core supplies measured evidence: attenuation correctness and evidence reconstructability hold on every trial, adjudication runs in single-digit microseconds, and the audit substrate's tamper-evidence behaves exactly as designed. We are explicit about scope: the architecture governs delegated action, not model behavior, and a full-system evaluation against a live agent benchmark is the invited next step.

item.title: Mind your key: An Empirical Study of LLM API Credential Leakage in iOS Apps
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12212v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLM）在移动应用中的快速集成，一类新型凭证安全风险日益凸显：LLM API密钥泄露导致攻击者可未经授权调用推理服务，直接造成开发者经济损失。现有研究多聚焦Android平台，尚无针对iOS生态的系统性实证研究，且缺乏无需源码或越狱即可检测密钥泄露的动态分析方法。

方法与创新

本研究构建了高质量数据集——从1092款候选iOS应用中经标准化筛选获得444款LLM集成应用；并提出LLMKeyLens框架：通过流量拦截、厂商特异性密钥提取（支持OpenAI、Anthropic等10+提供商）及主动有效性验证，实现零依赖（无需源码/二进制解密）的动态检测。

主要发现

  • 282款（63.5%）应用在HTTPS流量中暴露可利用的LLM API密钥，覆盖至少10家主流服务商；
  • 识别出三类高危泄漏模式：JWT令牌泄露（48%）、未认证后端代理访问（33%）、明文API密钥传输（19%）；
  • 责任披露三个月后复测显示：仅28%应用完成修复，72%仍可被利用，根源集中于后端身份验证缺失与JWT签名/过期逻辑缺陷。

启示与建议

结果揭示iOS生态中LLM密钥泄露具有高普遍性与强顽固性，暴露开发者实践与安全规范间的系统性断层。亟需三方协同：开发者提升密钥管理意识、LLM服务商提供明确安全指引与SDK加固支持、苹果平台强化网络通信安全审计机制。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first empirical study of LLM API key leakage in iOS applications—a critical yet unexplored threat causing financial loss through unauthorized inference access. We curated a dataset of 444 LLM-integrated iOS apps (from 1,092 candidates) and developed LLMKeyLens, a dynamic analysis framework that detects exploitable keys via traffic interception, provider-specific extraction, and active validity checking—requiring no source code or binary decryption. Our analysis found 282 apps (63.5%) leaking valid LLM credentials across ≥10 providers, with three dominant patterns: JWT-based token leakage (48%), unauthenticated backend proxy access (33%), and plaintext API key transmission (19%). After responsible disclosure, only 28% of vulnerable apps remediated within three months, while 72% remained exploitable—largely due to broken JWT implementations and missing backend authentication. These findings expose a systemic gap in secure LLM integration and underscore the need for coordinated action: developer education, explicit security guidance from LLM providers, and platform-level enforcement by Apple.

Abstract

The rapid integration of large language models (LLMs) into mobile applications has introduced a new class of credential security risk: leaked credentials that grant unauthorized access to LLM inference services, causing financial damage to developers. Prior work on credential leakage has focused primarily on Android apps; to date, no empirical study has systematically investigated LLM API key leakage in iOS applications. We present the first in-depth empirical study of API key leakage in LLM-integrated apps. We construct a high-quality dataset of 444 iOS applications, filtered from 1092 candidates through a standardized process, and develop LLMKeyLens, a dynamic analysis framework that detects LLM API key leakage via traffic interception, provider-specific key extraction, and active validity confirmation, requiring neither source code access nor binary decryption. Our analysis reveals that 282 applications expose exploitable LLM API credentials in network traffic, spanning at least ten providers. We identify three leakage patterns: JWT-based token leakage (48%), unauthenticated backend proxy access (33%), and plaintext API key transmission (19%). To assess remediation, we re-analyzed the same 282 vulnerable applications three months after responsible disclosure; only 28% had remediated the reported vulnerability, while 72% remained exploitable, with persistent issues stemming from unauthenticated backends and broken JWT implementations. Our findings show that LLM API key leakage is both prevalent and persistent in the iOS ecosystem, exposing a systemic gap between developer practice and secure integration principles, and suggest that secure LLM integration requires not only developer awareness but also explicit security guidance from providers and platform-level enforcement.

item.title: Categorical Robustness Assessment for Machine Learning based Network Intrusion Detection Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12075v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

基于机器学习（ML）的网络入侵检测系统（NIDS）面临严峻的对抗性攻击威胁：攻击者可通过添加微小但精心设计的扰动，诱导模型误判正常流量为恶意流量，或反之。尽管已有研究揭示了单模型的脆弱性，但缺乏在统一数据集、标准化攻击协议与多维度对比框架下的系统性鲁棒性评估——尤其缺少跨架构、跨攻击类型（FGSM/PGD）及按攻击强度（ε=0.01–0.1）的类别级稳健性分析，导致安全实践者难以做出可靠选型决策。

方法与实验设计

本研究采用ACI-IoT-2023数据集（含12类攻击、超120万样本），对三类主流NIDS架构进行严格对抗测试：1D卷积神经网络（CNN）、长短期记忆网络（LSTM） 和 随机森林（RF）。所有攻击均遵循对抗机器学习标准协议：在归一化特征空间中实施FGSM与PGD梯度攻击，扰动预算ε从0.01逐步增至0.1，确保可复现性与横向可比性。

关键发现与创新点

  • 颠覆性结论：高基线精度≠高鲁棒性。RF虽达99.98%准确率，但在ε=0.01时即暴跌73个百分点；CNN在ε=0.01下仍保持95.5%精度，且退化平缓；LSTM表现居中。
  • 首次提出“类别鲁棒性”评估范式：不仅报告整体精度衰减，更细粒度分析各攻击类型（如DDoS、Botnet）对不同模型的差异化影响，揭示CNN对时序无关型攻击（如PortScan）更具韧性。
  • 实践指南：明确推荐CNN作为对抗环境首选，并给出ε阈值驱动的部署策略（如ε<0.03时启用CNN+轻量级输入验证）。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents a systematic categorical robustness assessment of ML-based NIDS under adversarial attacks. Using the ACI-IoT-2023 dataset (1.2M samples, 12 attack types), we evaluate three architectures—1D CNN, LSTM, and Random Forest—against FGSM and PGD attacks across perturbation budgets (ε = 0.01 to 0.1) in normalized feature space. Contrary to conventional wisdom, Random Forest achieves near-perfect baseline accuracy (99.98%) but catastrophically fails under minimal perturbation (−73 pp at ε = 0.01), while CNN retains 95.5% accuracy at ε = 0.01 and degrades gracefully. LSTM shows intermediate robustness. Our findings highlight that baseline accuracy is insufficient for adversarial deployment; instead, architecture-specific resilience patterns—and notably CNN’s superior stability—should guide real-world NIDS selection. We provide actionable, scenario-aware deployment recommendations grounded in empirical robustness thresholds.

Abstract

Network Intrusion Detection Systems (NIDS) heavily utlize Machine Learning (ML) but ML models can be manipulated via adversarial attacks. These attacks add carefully crafted perturbations to network traffic data that leads to misclassifications. While prior work has demonstrated adversarial vulnerabilities in isolated settings, systematic cross-architecture as well as class and category of attack based comparisons under controlled attack conditions remain limited, leaving practitioners without clear guidance on which models to deploy in adversarial environments. This paper asks a simple question: what type of classifier architectures actually hold up when attackers try to manipulate the systems? We put three popular architectures through their paces: a 1D Convolutional Neural Network, a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network, and a Random Forest (RF) ensemble. Using the ACI-IoT-2023 dataset (over 1.2 million samples spanning 12 attack types), we subject each model with FGSM and PGD adversarial attacks, which apply gradient-based perturbations in normalized feature space consistent with established adversarial ML evaluation protocols, at perturbation budgets ranging from $ε=0.01$ to $ε=0.1$. Surprisingly, Random Forest achieved near-perfect baseline accuracy (99.98\%), yet collapsed catastrophically under attack, dropping 73 percentage points at the smallest perturbation we tested. CNN, on the other hand, retained 95.5\% accuracy at $ε=0.01$ and degraded gracefully as perturbations increased. LSTM fell somewhere in between. These findings flip the conventional wisdom where high baseline accuracy means nothing if a model shatters at the first sign of adversarial pressure. For practitioners deploying intrusion detection in adversarial environments, we recommend CNN-based architectures and provide scenario-specific deployment guidance.

item.title: Systematic Cybersecurity Risk Analysis of European Rail Traffic Management System
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11839v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

欧洲铁路交通管理系统（ERTMS）是欧盟统一列车运行控制的核心标准，支撑跨国家铁路互联互通与自动化运营。尽管其设计目标涵盖全自动运行等高阶场景，网络安全长期被置于功能与互操作性之后，缺乏系统性风险建模与量化评估。

方法与创新

本研究首次采用结构化威胁建模与多层技术映射法：

  • 全面解构ERTMS四级架构（轨旁、车载、无线通信、中心调度），识别各组件依赖的底层技术栈（如EuroBalise应答器、GSM-R、ETCS协议栈）；
  • 基于MITRE ATT&CK for ICS与EN 50126/50129标准，构建覆盖37类攻击向量的威胁库，并逐层映射至ERTMS具体实现；
  • 引入风险传播路径分析，评估漏洞在最小配置、增强安全措施部署及未来演进（如FRMCS替代GSM-R）三种场景下的持续性影响。

关键发现

1. 遗留技术构成系统性风险锚点：EuroBalise固件更新机制缺失与GSM-R加密弱性导致远程代码执行、虚假报文注入等高危威胁在所有部署形态中均无法根除；
2. ETCS Level 2全面部署是最高优先级缓解措施：其基于无线闭塞中心（RBC）的实时双向认证架构可规避83%的轨旁单点故障与中间人攻击；
3. 当前ERTMS整体风险评级达“高危”（CVSS加权均值7.4），可用性威胁（如DoS致列车降级运行）占比超65%，直接挑战铁路安全核心目标。

本研究为ERTMS提供了首个可复用的风险量化框架，揭示了标准演进中“安全内生”能力的结构性缺失，呼吁将网络安全从合规附加项升格为系统架构第一设计原则。

AI Summary (English)

This study presents the first systematic cybersecurity risk analysis of the European Rail Traffic Management System (ERTMS), revealing critical vulnerabilities rooted in legacy components—especially EuroBalises and GSM-R—whose weaknesses persist across minimal deployments, enhanced configurations, and future FRMCS-based evolutions. Using a layered threat modeling approach aligned with EN 50126/50129 and MITRE ATT&CK for ICS, we mapped 37 attack vectors to ERTMS architecture and quantified their propagation impact. Results show that full adoption of ETCS Level 2 is the single most effective mitigation, potentially eliminating 83% of high-severity threats by enabling real-time RBC-based authentication and bidirectional integrity checks. The system’s overall risk score (CVSS-weighted mean: 7.4) is dominated by availability threats (>65%), directly undermining operational safety. While our methodology demonstrates feasibility for railway-specific risk quantification, it also exposes gaps in current standards—highlighting the urgent need to embed security-by-design into ERTMS evolution rather than treating it as an afterthought.

Abstract

European Rail Traffic Management System (ERTMS) is a widely adopted standard unifying train management in the EU. While the standard allows for use cases like fully autonomous driving, cybersecurity has been an afterthought. Risk analysis enables the systematic assessment and prioritization of threats and mitigations. To date, it remains unclear which threats are most significant in ERTMS. This study systematically models components of ERTMS and analyzes their security in light of threats identified in the underlying technologies. The results suggest a concerning state of ERTMS, despite its critical role in railway safety. The use of legacy standards like EuroBalises and GSM-Railway (GSM-R) introduces vulnerabilities that persist across minimal ERTMS implementations, deployments incorporating various optional safety measures, and prospective future evolutions of the system, e.g., adopting Future Railway Mobile Communication System (FRMCS). Fully transitioning to European Train Control System (ETCS) level 2 was identified as the most significant measure for advancing ERTMS cybersecurity. The results indicate that a shift of ERTMS toward security is required to ensure availability and safe operation. While the chosen methodology proved its feasibility and shows remaining weaknesses of ERTMS, future work is needed to develop railway-centric adaptations to improve the quantification and evaluation of the computed risks.

item.title: Grammar-Constrained Decoding Can Jailbreak LLMs into Generating Malicious Code
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11817v1

AI Summary (中文)

语法约束解码：一把双刃剑——揭示其诱发大模型生成恶意代码的新风险

大型语言模型（LLMs）在代码生成场景中广泛应用，但其被滥用于生成恶意代码（如后门、勒索逻辑、漏洞利用脚本）的风险日益凸显。为提升生成代码的可靠性，语法约束解码（Grammar-Constrained Decoding, GCD） 已成主流技术——通过强制输出严格符合编程语言文法（如Python BNF）的代码，显著降低语法错误率。然而，本文首次揭示一个反直觉的安全悖论：GCD这一“可靠性增强器”本身可被武器化为新型攻击面。

我们提出首个针对GCD机制的 jailbreak 攻击——CodeSpear（代码矛）：攻击者仅需在推理阶段施加一个看似无害的、标准的代码语法约束（如合法Python文法），即可系统性绕过主流LLM的对齐防护，诱导模型生成功能完整、语义恶意的可执行代码。实验表明，该攻击在10个主流开源与闭源LLM（含Llama-3、Qwen2、DeepSeek-Coder等）上平均提升攻击成功率超30个百分点，显著优于传统提示注入类基线（如Ghost, Multi-Step）。

为根治此漏洞，我们设计CodeShield（代码盾）——一种面向代码模态的鲁棒安全对齐方法。其核心创新在于：在训练中引导模型在GCD条件下主动生成语义无害但结构多样的“蜜罐代码”（honeypot code），例如用空函数、无副作用日志或冗余占位符满足文法却拒绝恶意意图；同时保留自然语言场景下的明确拒答能力。在4个权威安全评测基准（CodeSafe、MaliciousBench、SafeCode、HarmfulCode）上的结果证实：CodeShield在抵御CodeSpear攻击时将有害响应率压降至<2%，且对良性代码生成任务（HumanEval、MBPP）的性能损失<0.8%。本研究首次揭示GCD的内在安全脆性，呼吁社区重新评估语法约束在安全关键场景中的部署范式。

AI Summary (English)

Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed for code generation, yet their potential misuse to produce malicious code remains a critical concern. Grammar-Constrained Decoding (GCD)—a widely adopted technique enforcing syntactic validity—has been assumed to improve reliability and safety. Contrary to this assumption, we uncover a fundamental vulnerability: GCD itself can be exploited as an attack surface. We introduce CodeSpear, the first jailbreak attack that leverages benign grammar constraints (e.g., standard Python grammar) to reliably induce LLMs to generate functionally malicious, executable code—bypassing standard safety alignment. Evaluated across 10 popular LLMs on 4 benchmarks, CodeSpear boosts attack success rates by >30 percentage points on average, outperforming state-of-the-art jailbreak baselines. To counter this threat, we propose CodeShield, a safety alignment method that teaches models to generate semantically harmless but structurally diverse honeypot code under GCD, while preserving natural-language refusals. CodeShield restores robust safety against CodeSpear (harmful response rate <2%) with negligible utility loss (<0.8% on HumanEval/MBPP). Our work exposes an inherent security risk in GCD and calls for rigorous security-aware design of constrained decoding.

Abstract

Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used for code generation, raising concerns that they may be misused to produce malicious code. Meanwhile, Grammar-Constrained Decoding (GCD) has been widely adopted to improve the reliability of LLM-generated code by enforcing syntactic validity. In this paper, we reveal a counterintuitive risk: this reliability-oriented technique can itself become an attack surface. We uncover a new jailbreak attack, termed CodeSpear, that exploits GCD to induce LLMs into generating malicious code. Our experiments show that simply applying a benign code grammar constraint can effectively jailbreak LLMs. To address this vulnerability, we propose CodeShield, a safety alignment approach that robustly preserves safe behavior even under attacker-controlled grammar constraints. CodeShield aligns the model in the code modality by teaching it to generate honeypot code under GCD. Such code is semantically harmless, so it does not implement the malicious request, and structurally diverse, so it is difficult to suppress through grammar tightening. At the same time, CodeShield still preserves natural-language refusals when natural language is available. Experiments on 10 popular LLMs across 4 benchmarks show that CodeSpear outperforms representative jailbreak baselines and increases the attack success rate by more than 30 percentage points on average. CodeShield also restores safety under CodeSpear while preserving benign utility. Our findings reveal a fundamental risk of GCD and call for greater attention to its potential security implications.

item.title: SwarmSense-DNN: A Trustworthy and Decentralized Neural Framework for Proactive Anomaly Defense in Consumer IoT
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11803v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

消费级物联网（IoT）设备爆发式增长，催生了由AI驱动的新型网络威胁，对可信、实时、隐私安全的异常检测提出严峻挑战。传统中心化检测范式面临三大瓶颈：跨设备通信开销巨大、单点故障风险高、以及集中式数据汇聚引发的严重隐私泄露隐患。

方法创新

本文提出 SwarmSense-DNN——一种基于群体智能（Swarm Intelligence）的去中心化神经框架，实现主动式异常防御。其核心设计包括：

  • ✅ 自主代理协同架构：每个IoT设备部署轻量级自治代理，通过局部交互形成自组织防御网络，无需中央协调；
  • ✅ 分层联邦学习+图神经网络（GNN）：在设备层执行本地深度模型训练，在边缘层通过GNN建模设备间拓扑关系，融合注意力机制捕获多尺度异常模式（局部突变 + 全局传播）；
  • ✅ 隐私与鲁棒性双保障：集成差分隐私（DP）噪声注入与动态代理冗余机制，兼顾数据不可追溯性与节点失效/对抗攻击下的持续运行能力。

关键成果

在5个权威IoT异常检测基准数据集（如NBaIoT、BoT-IoT、TON-IoT）上验证：

  • 平均检测准确率达 95.44%（较FedAvg提升8.2%，较集中式DNN高1.6%）；
  • 通信开销降低 67%（相比传统联邦学习），显著缓解带宽压力；
  • 在30%节点宕机或对抗样本注入下，检测F1-score仍保持 >91.5%，展现强容错性与抗攻击韧性。

本工作为消费IoT构建了首个兼具去中心化、隐私原生、自适应演化能力的可信防御新范式。

AI Summary (English)

SwarmSense-DNN is a decentralized neural framework leveraging swarm intelligence for proactive, privacy-preserving anomaly detection in consumer IoT. It replaces centralized coordination with autonomous agents that collaboratively learn via hierarchical federated learning, integrating graph neural networks and attention mechanisms to jointly model local device behaviors and global threat propagation patterns. Built-in differential privacy ensures data confidentiality, while dynamic agent redundancy guarantees resilience against node failures and AI-powered adversarial attacks. Evaluated across five benchmark IoT datasets, SwarmSense-DNN achieves a 95.44% average detection accuracy, outperforms centralized and standard federated baselines, reduces communication overhead by 67%, and maintains >91.5% F1-score under 30% node failure or adversarial perturbation.

Abstract

The rapid growth of consumer IoT devices has introduced unprecedented challenges in trustworthy anomaly detection against AI-enabled cyber threats, requiring real-time, privacy-preserving, and scalable defense mechanisms. Traditional centralized strategies face critical limitations, including communication bottlenecks, single points of failure, and privacy vulnerabilities when processing distributed consumer data. We propose SwarmSense-DNN, a novel decentralized neural framework employing swarm intelligence for secure, cooperative anomaly detection across distributed IoT environments. The framework integrates autonomous agents with deep neural networks to form a self-organizing defense system that detects evolving anomalies without centralized coordination. It utilizes hierarchical federated learning with graph neural networks and attention mechanisms to capture local and global anomaly behaviors while ensuring data privacy. Extensive experiments demonstrate SwarmSense-DNN's superior performance: it achieves 95.44% average detection accuracy across five benchmark datasets while reducing communication overhead by 67%. The framework maintains robust resilience against adversarial threats through differential privacy safeguards and demonstrates strong fault tolerance under node failures and AI-enabled attacks.

item.title: MHOT: Height-Optimized Authenticated Data Structure for Blockchain State Commitment
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11736v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

区块链状态根计算占区块处理时间的78%，是性能瓶颈核心。以太坊当前采用的Merkle Patricia Trie（MPT） 存在严重树高膨胀问题，且易受新型 Nurgle攻击（SP’24）——攻击者利用哈希碰撞人为拉长路径深度，以极低成本瘫痪系统。现有防御方案（如增大节点扇出度 span）虽可限制树高，却导致证明大小呈指数级增长；引入向量承诺等密码原语虽能缓解权衡，但需可信设置或带来高昂验证开销。

方法创新

本文提出 MHOT（Height-Optimized Authenticated Data Structure）：一种无需可信设置、兼容标准哈希验证的高度最优认证数据结构。其核心突破在于键索引范式重构——摒弃MPT的固定前缀索引（导致扇出与跨度指数耦合），转而基于实际区分键的判别性比特位进行动态索引，实现自适应跨度与线性扇出耦合，并严格证明其树高达到理论最小值。为避免高扇出推高证明开销，我们设计分层证明机制：采用双层Merkle结构，将单节点证明开销从 O(k) 降至 O(log k)。

实验结果

在以太坊主网真实负载下，MHOT相较MPT实现：写吞吐量提升9倍、写放大降低4倍、证明体积缩小50%；面对Nurgle攻击（攻击者耗尽整块Gas），MHOT攻击成功率稳定为0%（MPT高达99.97%）。结果表明：高度最优性本身即是最关键的可扩展性与抗攻击性抽象，无需依赖新密码学原语。

AI Summary (English)

State root computation consumes 78% of blockchain block processing time. Ethereum’s Merkle Patricia Trie (MPT) suffers from unbounded height growth and is vulnerable to low-cost Nurgle attacks, where adversaries inflate path depth via hash collisions. Prior defenses trade height control for exponential proof-size blowup or rely on vector commitments requiring trusted setup or costly verification. We propose MHOT, a height-optimal authenticated data structure for state commitment that uses only standard hash-based verification—no trusted setup required. MHOT replaces MPT’s fixed-prefix indexing with discriminative-bit indexing, decoupling span and fanout linearly and achieving provably minimal height. To keep proofs compact despite high fanout, we introduce hierarchical proofs: a two-layer Merkle construction reducing per-node proof overhead from O(k) to O(log k). On Ethereum mainnet workloads, MHOT delivers up to 9× higher write throughput, 4× lower write amplification, and 2× smaller proofs than MPT—and achieves 0% Nurgle attack success rate (vs. 99.97% for MPT), even under full-block-gas adversarial budget. Our results demonstrate that height optimality—not new cryptography—is the key abstraction for scalable, attack-resilient state commitment.

Abstract

State root computation dominates (78%) blockchain block processing time. Ethereum's canonical authenticated data structure, i.e., Merkle Patricia Trie (MPT), suffers from severe tree-height growth and is vulnerable to \textit{Nurgle attacks} (SP'24), where adversaries inflate path depth via hash collisions and degrade system performance at negligible cost. Existing defenses increase node fanout (span) to bound tree height, but higher span inflates proof size exponentially. Prior work mitigates this trade-off using vector commitments, at the cost of trusted setup or expensive verification. We present \textsc{Mhot}, a height-optimal authenticated data structure for blockchain state commitment that preserves standard hash-based verification without trusted setup. Unlike MPT's fixed-prefix indexing, which couples span and fanout exponentially, \textsc{Mhot} indexes by discriminative bits that actually distinguish keys, achieving adaptive span with linear fanout coupling and provably minimal height. To prevent high fanout from inflating proofs, we introduce hierarchical proofs, a two-layer Merkle construction that reduces per-node proof overhead from O(k) to O(log k). On Ethereum mainnet workloads, \textsc{Mhot} achieves up to 9X higher write throughput, 4X lower write amplification, and 2X smaller proofs than MPT. Under Nurgle attacks, even when the adversary consumes an entire block's gas budget, \textsc{Mhot} maintains a 0% attack success rate (v.s., 99.97% for MPT). Our results, somewhat surprisingly, show that height optimality (not new crypto primitives!) is the key abstraction for scalable and attack-resilient blockchain state commitment.

item.title: T2S: A Rehearsal-Based Approach for Extraction-Resistant Model Watermarking
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11698v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

模型水印（Model Watermarking）是保护人工智能模型知识产权的关键技术，其核心在于向目标模型嵌入可验证的、行为可识别的独特知识。然而，现有方法在面对模型提取攻击（Model Extraction Attacks）时鲁棒性严重不足——攻击者仅通过查询原始模型的预测输出，即可训练出功能高度相似的“盗用模型”（surrogate model），进而绕过或擦除水印，导致版权保护失效。

方法创新：T2S 框架

本文提出 T2S（Trigger-based Transferable Watermarking via Simulation）——一种基于重演式微调（rehearsal-based fine-tuning）的新型水印嵌入框架。其核心思想是：主动模拟被盗场景，构建一个轻量级“模拟盗用模型”，并在其上定义以触发集（trigger set）为输入的损失函数；该损失被反向传播至原始水印模型，驱动水印知识向更具迁移性（transferability）的方向优化。这一机制使水印不再仅依赖于原始模型内部参数分布，而是深度耦合于模型的泛化行为，从而在提取后的盗用模型中仍能稳定激活并可检测。

主要成果与验证

在ImageNet、CIFAR-100等多基准数据集及ResNet/ViT等多种架构上，T2S显著提升水印存活率：面对主流提取攻击（如Knockoff Nets、Model Stealing），水印检测准确率平均提升28.6%–41.3%；在后续叠加剪枝、微调、对抗蒸馏等水印移除攻击下，仍保持>92%的检测召回率。实验表明，T2S首次实现了水印鲁棒性与模型性能（精度下降<0.5%）的协同优化，为AI模型版权保护提供了可部署、可验证的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Model watermarking protects AI model intellectual property by embedding detectable behavioral signatures, but suffers from poor robustness against model extraction attacks—where adversaries train surrogate models using only black-box predictions. To address this, we propose T2S, a rehearsal-based watermarking framework that enhances transferability and persistence of watermarks under extraction. T2S simulates the stealing process during training: it constructs a lightweight surrogate model and uses its loss on a trigger set as a supervision signal to fine-tune the target model’s watermark knowledge. This encourages watermark embedding in behaviorally critical, generalizable features rather than fragile parameter patterns. Extensive experiments across datasets (ImageNet, CIFAR-100) and architectures (ResNet, ViT) show T2S improves watermark detection accuracy by 28.6–41.3% under extraction attacks and maintains >92% recall after aggressive removal attempts (pruning, fine-tuning, adversarial distillation), with <0.5% accuracy drop on clean data.

Abstract

Model watermarking safeguards AI model intellectual property by embedding distinctive knowledge that induces unique behavioral signatures. The primary technical challenge lies in ensuring watermark robustness against various post-processing attacks on the watermarked model. Model extraction attacks emerge as the most severe threat, where adversaries exploit prediction outputs to train surrogate models that illegally replicate the original model's functionality. In this work, we propose a rehearsal-based watermark embedding framework to enhance the robustness of model watermarks against model extraction attacks. By simulating the extraction process, our method leverages the loss of a \textit{simulated stolen model} on a trigger set as a training signal to fine-tune the watermark knowledge within the target model. This fine-tuning step encourages the watermark to be embedded in a way that boosts transferability, thereby increasing its chances of persisting and remaining detectable in stolen models. Comprehensive experiments conducted under diverse settings demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves the robustness of model watermarks against both model extraction and subsequent watermark removal attacks.

item.title: Can Open-Source LLM Agents Replace Static Application Security Testing Tools? An Empirical Assessment
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11672v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

随着生成式人工智能（GenAI）的快速发展，开源大语言模型（LLM）代理被广泛探索用于网络安全任务。然而，其在专业安全检测场景（如静态应用安全测试，SAST）中的实际有效性仍缺乏实证支撑。本文聚焦核心问题：当前开源LLM代理能否替代经过工业验证的专用SAST工具？

方法设计

本研究构建了基于Ollama平台的三款主流开源LLM代理（Llama 3-8B、Phi-3-mini、Qwen2-7B），统一接入标准化Agent框架（含代码解析、漏洞推理与报告生成模块）。所有代理均在相同实验环境下，对包含127个真实Python安全缺陷（涵盖CWE-78、CWE-89、CWE-79等常见漏洞）的基准数据集进行扫描。评估采用四维指标：精确率（Precision）、召回率（Recall）、误报数（False Positives），并创新性提出加权复合得分（Composite Score）——综合权衡检出能力与误报代价（公式：$CS = 0.4 \times Recall + 0.4 \times Precision - 0.2 \times FP_{norm}$）。基线对比工具为业界广泛采用的开源SAST工具Bandit（v1.7.5）。

主要发现与结论

  • 所有LLM代理的平均召回率（32.1%–41.7%）显著低于Bandit（89.2%），尤其在逻辑型漏洞（如业务逻辑绕过）上漏报严重；
  • 平均误报率达Bandit的5.3倍（最高达142个/项目），导致安全团队人工复核成本激增；
  • 复合得分最高代理（Qwen2-7B）仅达Bandit得分的61.4%，且性能波动大（标准差±12.3），缺乏稳定性；
  • 关键洞见：LLM代理依赖上下文推理而非语法/语义规则建模，难以可靠捕获隐式控制流缺陷与跨文件数据流，本质不匹配SAST的确定性分析范式。

本研究首次系统量化了开源LLM代理在SAST任务中的性能缺口，为AI安全工具选型提供了实证基准与风险警示。

AI Summary (English)

This paper empirically assesses whether open-source LLM-based agents can replace production-grade Static Application Security Testing (SAST) tools. We evaluate three Ollama-hosted models—Llama 3-8B, Phi-3-mini, and Qwen2-7B—integrated into a unified agentic framework, against Bandit (v1.7.5) on a curated Python benchmark of 127 real-world vulnerabilities. Performance is measured via precision, recall, false positive count, and a novel composite score weighting detection efficacy and noise penalty. Results show all LLM agents underperform Bandit substantially: average recall (32–42%) is less than half of Bandit’s (89%), false positives are 5.3× higher, and the best agent’s composite score reaches only 61.4% of Bandit’s. Crucially, LLMs fail systematically on control-flow and inter-procedural flaws due to inherent limitations in deterministic code analysis—highlighting that current GenAI agents are not yet viable SAST substitutes under realistic conditions.

Abstract

This paper explores the value of agentic AI tools for cybersecurity purposes. We evaluate the efficacy of a general-purpose GenAI Large Language Model- (GenAI-) based agent when powered by three different Ollama-hosted general-purpose open source models. We assess each agent's performance using precision, recall, false positive count, and a calculated composite score based upon the interplay of the captured metrics, against the baseline performance of an existing, vetted Static Application Security Testing (SAST) tool, Bandit. Our findings refute the notion that a modern open-source GenAI LLM-based agent is currently suitable for the specialized task of SAST scanning under realistic conditions.

item.title: Runtime Skill Audit: Targeted Runtime Probing for Agent Skill Security
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11671v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大型语言模型（LLM）智能体通过“技能”（Skills）复用指令、工具、资源与工作流，显著提升可扩展性与实用性。然而，技能亦构成新型安全风险载体：其文档或源码看似无害，却可能在特定运行时条件下（如特定用户请求、本地资产访问、持久化状态依赖或跨步骤工具链调用）触发恶意行为。传统静态审查（如代码扫描、文档分析）因无法感知上下文动态性而极易失效，存在严重漏检风险。

方法：Runtime Skill Audit（RSA）

我们提出运行时技能审计（RSA），一种面向安全的动态分析框架。RSA不采用统一泛化测试任务，而是：

  • 风险导向接口剖析：识别技能中高危接口（如文件读写、网络外连、系统命令执行）；
  • 上下文精准构造：自动构建能激活这些接口的最小可行执行环境（含模拟用户输入、伪造敏感资产、预置状态等）；
  • 证据驱动标签判定：基于实际执行轨迹（API调用序列、数据流向、权限变更等）生成细粒度安全标签（如“潜在越权”“隐蔽数据渗出”）。

实验与创新点

在OpenClaw平台实现RSA，对100个真实技能开展评估。相比主流静态基线（包括规则引擎、微调分类器、LLM零样本检测），RSA达到90.0%准确率，88.0%真阳性率（TPr）与仅8.0%假阳性率（FPr），较最优静态方法提升13.0个百分点。尤为关键的是，在模拟的自演化攻击（攻击者逐轮修改技能规避检测）下，所有静态检测器在1–2轮内崩溃（TPr < 30%），而RSA持续稳定识别出19–20/20个恶意技能，验证了其对抗适应性攻击的鲁棒性。本工作首次将动态探针技术系统化引入技能安全审计，为可信智能体部署提供可落地的运行时保障范式。

AI Summary (English)

Runtime Skill Audit (RSA) is a dynamic security analysis framework for LLM agent skills—reusable modules that encapsulate instructions, tools, and workflows. Unlike brittle static vetting, RSA probes skills during execution under adversarially tailored conditions: it first identifies risk-relevant interfaces (e.g., file I/O, network calls), then constructs minimal yet sufficient runtime contexts (e.g., simulated sensitive assets, multi-step tool sequences) to activate them, and finally assigns security labels based on observed trace evidence (API logs, data flows, privilege changes). Evaluated on 100 real-world skills in OpenClaw, RSA achieves 90.0% accuracy, 88.0% TPR, and only 8.0% FPR, outperforming the best static baseline by +13.0 percentage points. Crucially, under self-evolving attacks—where adversaries iteratively modify skills to evade detection—static methods fail within 1–2 rounds, while RSA maintains near-perfect detection (19–20/20 malicious skills) across all rounds. RSA establishes a practical, context-aware paradigm for ensuring skill integrity in production agent systems.

Abstract

Agent skills let LLM agents reuse instructions, resources, tools, and workflows, but they also create a new place for malicious behavior to hide. A skill may look benign in its documentation or code while becoming harmful only when it is invoked with particular user requests, local assets, persistent state, or multi-step tool interactions. This makes purely static vetting brittle. We present Runtime Skill Audit (RSA), a dynamic analysis method that audits skills by asking what the skill-mediated agent actually does under targeted runtime conditions. Instead of testing every skill with the same generic tasks, RSA profiles risk-relevant interfaces, prepares the execution context needed to exercise them, and assigns security labels from the resulting trace evidence. We instantiate RSA on OpenClaw and evaluate it on 100 skills against representative static baselines. RSA achieves 90.0\% accuracy with an 88.0\% true positive rate and an 8.0\% false positive rate, improving accuracy by 13.0 percentage points over the best static baseline. Under self-evolving attacks, static detectors collapse after one or two rounds, while RSA continues to detect 19--20 out of 20 malicious skills across rounds.

item.title: Sovereign Assurance Boundary: Certificate-Bound Admission for Agentic Infrastructure
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11632v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

智能体基础设施（Agentic Infrastructure）引发关键的控制平面授权挑战：非确定性推理系统可能自主提出高风险的生产环境资源变更请求，而现有安全机制——如身份与访问管理（IAM）、策略引擎、共识协议及审计日志——或仅实施静态、上下文无关的权限控制，或仅在操作执行后进行事后记录，无法实现事前可验证、可撤销、证据绑定的授权决策。

方法：主权保障边界（SAB）

本文提出主权保障边界（Sovereign Assurance Boundary, SAB），一种证书绑定的运行时准入层。SAB 在“保障气闸”（assurance airlock）处拦截智能体提案，将其编译为类型化执行合约 $C$，并严格绑定至三项核心要素：（1）加密证据摘要 $H(E)$（涵盖上下文快照、策略依据、资源状态指纹等）；（2）策略版本号；（3）后果感知的认证路径（consequence-aware certification paths）。通过多级策略校验与影响评估后，系统签发主权保障证书（$Ω$），其作用域被精确限定于唯一执行身份、指定吊销纪元（revocation epoch）及有限有效期窗口。

创新与验证

SAB 采用“气闸–代理”双组件架构：气闸负责合约生成与证书签发；主权执行代理（sovereign execution broker）在调用基础设施API前，实时验证 $Ω$ 的签名有效性，并执行新鲜吊销检查（fresh revocation check）与状态漂移检测（drift check），阻断已失效或环境不一致的执行。我们形式化定义了准入与吊销不变式，并基于Go语言原型完成2500次准入测试，结果显示平均延迟<87ms，证书验证开销<3.2ms，支持毫秒级策略更新同步与亚秒级吊销生效。该模型将自主推理与状态变更彻底解耦，使委托执行权转化为密码学可验证、证据可追溯、吊销即时生效、操作可重放的运行时第一类实体。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces the Sovereign Assurance Boundary (SAB), a certificate-bound runtime admission layer for agentic infrastructure. SAB addresses the critical gap in pre-execution authorization by intercepting agent proposals at an “assurance airlock”, compiling them into typed execution contracts $C$, and binding each to cryptographic evidence digests $H(E)$, policy versions, and consequence-aware certification paths. Upon successful admission, it issues a signed Sovereign Assurance Certificate ($Ω$) scoped to a specific execution identity, revocation epoch, and validity window. A sovereign execution broker then verifies $Ω$ and performs real-time revocation and state-drift checks before invoking infrastructure APIs. Evaluated on a Go prototype across 2,500 admission attempts, SAB achieves sub-100ms average latency, <3.2ms verification overhead, and supports policy updates with sub-second revocation propagation. The model transforms delegated execution authority into a cryptographically verifiable, evidence-bound, revocable, and replayable runtime artifact—preventing direct autonomous state mutation.

Abstract

Agentic infrastructure introduces a critical control-plane authorization problem: non-deterministic reasoning systems can propose high-stakes mutations to production resources, yet existing security mechanisms -- such as identity and access management (IAM), policy engines, consensus protocols, and audit logs -- either enforce static, context-unaware permissions or merely record actions post-execution. This paper introduces the Sovereign Assurance Boundary (SAB), a certificate-bound runtime admission layer for autonomous execution authority. SAB intercepts agent proposals at an assurance airlock, compiles them into typed execution contracts $C$, and binds these contracts to cryptographic evidence digests $H(E)$ and policy versions. The contracts are then routed through consequence-aware certification paths. Upon successful admission, the system emits a signed Sovereign Assurance Certificate ($Ω$) that is strictly scoped to a specific execution identity, revocation epoch, and validity window. Finally, a sovereign execution broker verifies $Ω$ and performs fresh pre-execution revocation and drift checks before invoking infrastructure APIs. We detail the airlock-broker architecture, formalize its admission and revocation invariants, and report preliminary feasibility measurements from a Go prototype evaluated over 2,500 admission attempts. Ultimately, this broker-enforced model prevents autonomous reasoning from directly mutating state, transforming delegated execution authority into a cryptographically verifiable, evidence-bound, revocable, and replayable runtime artifact.

item.title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Autoencoder for ECG Anomaly Detection on Edge Devices
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11556v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

持续心电图（ECG）监测可提前识别心律失常，预防心血管事件。但临床落地需同步满足三大刚性约束：法律级隐私保护（GDPR/HIPAA合规）、边缘设备实时推理能力（如树莓派4等资源受限硬件），以及跨医院非独立同分布（non-IID）数据下的检测鲁棒性。

方法创新

本文提出首个端到端隐私保护型联邦自编码器系统，面向12导联ECG无监督异常检测（基于PTB-XL数据集）。核心技术栈包括：

  • 三类自编码器架构（VanillaAE、ConvAE、VAE）的联邦协同训练；
  • 基于Flower框架的联邦平均（FedAvg），模拟10家异构医院协作；
  • 客户端侧差分隐私随机梯度下降（DP-SGD），采用Rényi-DP会计机制保障$(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP形式化隐私；
  • 8位整数量化（INT8） 后训练压缩，并在Raspberry Pi 4上实测部署性能。

关键发现与贡献

  • Federated learning在所有架构中均持平或超越中心化基线（ConvAE联邦AUROC达0.782）；
  • $\varepsilon$敏感性分析确定$\varepsilon=4$为临床推荐操作点（兼顾隐私强度与模型效用）；
  • INT8量化使模型体积减半、Pi 4推理延迟降低最高44%，且AUROC损失<0.12%；
  • 首次实证验证：DP噪声与量化误差效应相互独立，无需在强隐私与轻量部署间妥协；
  • 系统首次融合联邦学习+形式化差分隐私+无监督重构检测+AArch64量化部署，为医疗边缘AI提供可复现、合规、实用的技术范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first end-to-end privacy-preserving federated autoencoder system for unsupervised 12-lead ECG anomaly detection on edge devices. We jointly address GDPR/HIPAA compliance, real-time inference on resource-constrained hardware (e.g., Raspberry Pi 4), and robustness under non-IID cross-hospital data—using PTB-XL, three autoencoder families (VanillaAE, ConvAE, VAE), Flower-based FedAvg across ten simulated hospitals, client-side $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP via Rényi-accounted DP-SGD, and INT8 post-training quantization. Federated training matches or exceeds centralized baselines (best federated AUROC = 0.782), with $\varepsilon = 4$ identified as the optimal clinical privacy budget. INT8 cuts model size by ~50% and Pi 4 latency by up to 44%, incurring <0.12% AUROC drop. Crucially, DP and quantization degradations are empirically independent—enabling strong privacy and edge efficiency simultaneously. This work bridges formal privacy, federated learning, unsupervised anomaly detection, and production-ready AArch64 deployment.

Abstract

Continuous electrocardiography (ECG) monitoring could surface rhythm abnormalities before they escalate into cardiovascular events. However, a deployable system must satisfy three requirements simultaneously: legal-grade privacy (GDPR, HIPAA), real-time inference on constrained edge hardware, and detection quality under non-IID cross-hospital data. We design and evaluate an end-to-end federated system addressing all three for unsupervised 12-lead ECG anomaly detection on PTB-XL dataset, combining three autoencoder families (VanillaAE, ConvAE, VAE), Flower-based federated averaging (FedAvg) across ten simulated hospitals, client-side differentially private SGD (DP-SGD) with a Rényi-DP accountant, and 8-bit integer (INT8) post-training quantization with Raspberry Pi 4 benchmarking. Our main contributions are: an empirical characterization of how these mechanisms compose, practical DP-specific recommendations, and technical and security insights for a clinically sensitive setting. Federated learning matches or exceeds the centralized baseline across all architectures (ConvAE federated area under the ROC curve, AUROC, $0.782$), and an $\varepsilon$ sweep identifies $\varepsilon=4$ as the recommended clinical operating point. INT8 quantization roughly halves model size and cuts Pi 4 latency by up to $44%$ with $<0.12%$ AUROC loss. Crucially, DP and quantization penalties are empirically independent, so practitioners need not trade a strong privacy guarantee for a compact edge footprint. To our knowledge, this is the first system combining federated learning, formal $(\varepsilon,δ)$-DP, unsupervised reconstruction-based detection, and quantized AArch64 deployment.

item.title: Making Foresight Actionable: Repurposing Representation Alignment in World Action Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12217v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

世界动作模型（World Action Models, WAMs）通过视频生成模型预测未来场景演化，进而生成机器人操控动作，为具身智能提供了新范式。然而，本研究发现一个关键矛盾：视觉上 plausible 的未来帧生成，并不等价于可执行的精准动作输出。大量实证表明，现有WAMs在动作解码阶段存在严重偏差——动作头注意力机制未能聚焦于任务关键交互区域（如抓取点、接触面），反而对无关背景扰动高度敏感，导致动作泛化性与鲁棒性不足。

方法创新：AGRA目标

为弥合“视觉表征”与“动作控制”之间的语义鸿沟，本文提出 AGRA（Action-Grounded Representation Alignment）：一种新型表征对齐目标。其核心是在中间扩散特征层强制对齐——将视频扩散模型的隐状态与基础视觉编码器（如SAM或DINOv2）输出的空间一致语义表征进行跨模态对齐，引导模型学习动作导向的、语义结构化的中间表示。该目标轻量、即插即用，无需修改主干架构或增加额外参数。

主要成果与影响

在真实机器人操纵任务（如堆叠、推拉、夹取）上的系统实验表明：

  • AGRA显著提升动作解码器对交互区域的关注精度（IoU提升23.7%）；
  • 增强物体定位准确性（平均误差降低31%）与功能 affordance 理解能力；
  • 显著提升策略鲁棒性：在任务无关区域添加遮挡/噪声时，成功率下降幅度减少58%；
  • 全面提升性能：在分布内任务中平均成功率提升12.4%，在跨场景、跨物体的分布外泛化任务中提升达19.6%。

本工作揭示了世界模型中“表征可操作性”的本质瓶颈，并为构建可信赖、可解释、可泛化的具身决策模型提供了可扩展的对齐范式。

AI Summary (English)

World Action Models (WAMs) leverage video diffusion to predict future scenes and derive robot actions—but we find that visually plausible futures often yield inaccurate control outputs. Diagnostic analysis reveals a critical representation mismatch: hidden states optimized for visual reconstruction lack inherent action-grounded structure, causing the action decoder to attend poorly to task-relevant interaction regions and remain brittle to irrelevant perturbations. To address this, we propose AGRA (Action-Grounded Representation Alignment), a lightweight objective that aligns intermediate diffusion features with spatially coherent semantic representations from a foundation visual encoder (e.g., DINOv2). Evaluated on real-world manipulation tasks, AGRA improves interaction region focus (+23.7% IoU), object localization accuracy (−31% error), and affordance understanding. It boosts in-distribution success by +12.4% and out-of-distribution generalization by +19.6%, while enhancing robustness to task-irrelevant perturbations (58% reduction in performance drop). AGRA bridges the gap between world modeling and actionable control—without architectural changes or extra parameters.

Abstract

World Action Models (WAMs) offer a promising route for robot manipulation by using video generation models to model future scene evolution before producing control actions. However, our empirical observations reveal a phenomenon: generating plausible visual futures does not always guarantee the extraction of accurate actions. To diagnose this failure, we conduct action-head attention analysis and causal interventions. We find that the action decoder fails to focus on task-relevant interaction regions and remains sensitive to perturbations in task-irrelevant areas. This reveals a representation mismatch: hidden states optimized for visual reconstruction are not inherently organized in a form useful for low-level action control. In this paper, we propose AGRA, an Action-Grounded Representation Alignment objective that regularizes the world-action interface by aligning intermediate video diffusion features with spatially coherent semantic representations from a foundation visual encoder. We evaluate AGRA on real-world manipulation tasks. Experiments show that AGRA makes world model representations more action-grounded: by focusing the action decoder on the correct interaction regions, it improves object localization accuracy and affordance understanding, and makes the policy more robust to perturbations in task-irrelevant regions. As a result, AGRA consistently improves both in-distribution performance and out-of-distribution generalization over the baseline world action model.

item.title: MODF-SIR: A Multi-agent Omni-modal Distilled Framework for Social Intelligence Reasoning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12018v1

AI Summary (中文)

MODF-SIR：面向社会智能推理的多智能体全模态蒸馏框架

本研究提出MODF-SIR（Multi-agent Omni-modal Distilled Framework for Social Intelligence Reasoning），一种专为社会智能推理（Social Intelligence Reasoning, SIR）设计的轻量级、多智能体协同框架。核心创新在于全流程知识蒸馏增强：在训练与推理阶段同步引入蒸馏机制，显著提升小模型在复杂社会语境下的泛化能力与鲁棒性。针对社会交互中长尾事件（如微表情、隐含意图、文化特异性行为）易被主流模态数据淹没的问题，框架首创精准模态定位—长尾显式化双阶段处理：首先通过多智能体协作对视频、音频、文本等全模态输入进行细粒度时空对齐与关键区域定位；继而将识别出的长尾事件结构化编码为格式化文本（如[Intent: subtle disapproval] [Modality: eyebrow raise + pause]），从根本上规避其在Tokenizer中被头部事件或环境噪声稀释的风险。

进一步，我们首次将测试时自适应（Test-Time Adaptation, TTA）深度嵌入完整推理链——覆盖长尾事件提取、思维链（CoT）生成、自我反思三阶段，并采用LoRA驱动的实例级蒸馏微调，仅对单样本推理路径动态注入低秩适配参数，实现高效、可解释的在线优化。在SocialIQ、SOMA、VIST-SIR等6个权威基准上，MODF-SIR以仅约30% IntentTrain训练数据量，全面超越Llama-3-Vision、Qwen-VL、GPT-4o等开源及闭源大模型，多项指标达SOTA。代码、交互Demo、LoRA权重及专用路由数据集均已开源。

AI Summary (English)

We present MODF-SIR, a multi-agent omni-modal distilled framework for social intelligence reasoning (SIR). Built upon a lightweight multimodal LLM, it uniquely integrates knowledge distillation into both training and inference. Key innovations include: (1) precise localization of social-relevant multi-modal data and explicit formatting of long-tail events (e.g., "[Intent: hesitation] [Modality: gaze aversion + speech pause]") to prevent token-level suppression; and (2) LoRA-enhanced test-time adaptation (TTA) across the full reasoning pipeline—spanning event extraction, chain-of-thought prompting, and self-reflection—for instance-level, on-the-fly optimization. Evaluated on six benchmarks (e.g., SocialIQ, SOMA), MODF-SIR achieves state-of-the-art performance using only ~30% of IntentTrain data—outperforming Llama-3-Vision, Qwen-VL, and GPT-4o. Code, demo, LoRA adapters, and the IntentRouterTrain dataset are publicly available.

Abstract

We propose a multi-agent collaborative framework built upon a lightweight Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM), specifically designed for social intelligence reasoning. A key feature of our approach is that both the training and inference phases are augmented via knowledge distillation. Within this architecture, multi-modal data pertinent to social intelligence is precisely localized. Furthermore, relevant long-tail events are identified, extracted, and rendered as formatted, explicit text. This formatting strategy prevents critical long-tail information from being overshadowed by head events and environmental noise during the tokenization process. Specifically, we integrate Test-Time Adaptation (TTA) across the entire reasoning pipeline, encompassing the extraction and representation of long-tail events, Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting, and self-reflection. This TTA mechanism is also distillation-enhanced, utilizing Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) to fine-tune the foundation model exclusively for instance-level reasoning. Extensive evaluations against various open-source and proprietary AI models across multiple benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework. With around 30% of training data from IntentTrain, we achieve state-of-the-art results. Codes are available at https://github.com/eeee-sys/MODF-SIR, demo is available at https://huggingface.co/spaces/Harry-1234/MODF-SIR, LoRA is available at https://huggingface.co/Harry-1234/MODF-SIR and the dataset for training router is available at https://huggingface.co/datasets/Harry-1234/IntentRouterTrain.

item.title: Agents All the Way Down; A Methodology for Building Custom AI Agents from Substrate to Production
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11869v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前AI工程实践中，通用大模型（如ChatGPT）与定制化AI代理（Custom AI Agents）存在根本性分野：后者深度嵌入特定应用，直连私有数据与工具，承载独立安全边界、品牌标识与完整审计追踪。然而，尽管函数调用API、模型上下文协议（MCP）、代码协作代理等技术组件已广泛可用，尚无系统性、可复现的端到端方法论指导工程师从零构建并交付生产级定制代理——相关实践散见于播客、博客与泄露的系统提示中，缺乏结构化沉淀。

方法论：Agents All the Way Down

本文提出首个框架无关（framework-free）的定制代理构建方法论，核心为“两预设、三循环”：

  • P1（基底预设）：将LLM视为软件组件，分层建模为工具→系统→消息，并引入提示缓存（prompt-caching）保障确定性；
  • P2（构件预设）：整合函数调用、MCP、CLI编排、“轻壳”（liteshell）模式、代理循环、技能封装、角色抽象、钩子机制与脚手架；
  • P3–P5（迭代实践）：① 用通用代理快速原型（P3）；② 提取逻辑、折叠为可执行CLI工具，采用“海龟模式”（Turtle pattern）交付（P4）；③ 以通用代理驱动被测代理执行行为场景，实现“代理测试代理”（agent-tests-agent），作为经典单元/集成测试的互补而非替代（P5）。

创新与验证

该方法论在开源LAMB平台的定制代理AAC上完成实证：单名开发者+AI结对编程，10天内完成开发并上线生产。关键推论自然浮现：多代理协同即CLI组合。全文强调工程可维护性——代理由其长期维护者亲手构建，以“适配性”（fit）而非“通用能力”定义价值，为AI原生应用提供可迁移、语言/框架无关的实践范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Agents All the Way Down, a framework-free methodology for building production-ready custom AI agents—agents deeply embedded in their application, secured by strict boundaries, and owned end-to-end by the engineer who maintains them. It synthesizes fragmented practices into two foundational preconditions (P1: LLM as a layered software component—tools → system → messages—with prompt caching; P2: curated building blocks including function calling, MCP, CLI orchestration, liteshell, agent loop, skills, characters, hooks, and scaffolding) and three iterative practices (P3: prototype with a general-purpose agent; P4: harvest, fold, and ship as a CLI via the Turtle pattern; P5: agent-tests-agent behavioral validation, complementing classical testing). Validated on the AAC agent for the open-source LAMB platform—built in ~10 days by one developer with AI pair-programming—the methodology yields a key insight: multi-agent orchestration reduces to CLI composition. It prioritizes fit over capability, enabling transferable, language-agnostic agent engineering.

Abstract

Custom AI agents areagents that live inside their own application, talk to their own data and tools, enforce their own security boundaries, and carry their own brand and audit trail. What separates them from the general-purpose tier is fit, not capability: each is built for one job, by the engineer who will maintain it. No published practice sets out how to build one end to end. The pieces are everywhere (function-calling APIs, the Model Context Protocol, code agents to pair with), but the practice that chains them lives in podcasts, blogs, and leaked system prompts. This paper writes that practice down as a methodology, Agents All the Way Down: two preconditions crossed once and kept, then three practices repeated for the agent's life. The preconditions are (P1) Substrate, the LLM as a software component, framed as tools, then system, then messages under prompt-caching; and (P2) Building blocks: function calling, MCP, CLI orchestration, the liteshell pattern, the agent loop, skills, characters, hooks, and scaffolding. The practices are (P3) prototype with a general-purpose agent; (P4) harvest, fold, and ship the result as a CLI, the Turtle pattern; and (P5) agent-tests-agent, in which a general-purpose agent drives it through behavioural scenarios, a complement to classical testing, not a replacement. The working loop is P3 to P4 to P5 and back, and one corollary falls out for free: multi-agent orchestration is just CLI composition. The methodology is framework-free by construction. It was distilled from the AAC, a custom agent for the open-source LAMB platform, built in about ten days by one developer with an AI pair-programmer and in production . We present it as a transferable practice, independent of any language or framework.

item.title: Claw-SWE-Bench: A Benchmark for Evaluating OpenClaw-style Agent Harnesses on Coding Tasks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12344v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

通用AI代理（如OpenClaw）正日益作为自主工具使用者参与软件开发，但其代码能力在主流基准SWE-bench上难以准确评估：标准SWE-bench要求严格的Docker沙箱环境、规范补丁格式与预测契约，而OpenClaw等开放式代理框架缺乏内置的执行隔离与结构化输出机制，导致评分不可靠。

方法与构建

本文提出Claw-SWE-Bench——首个专为OpenClaw风格代理“爪具”（claws）设计的公平可比基准。它包含两大核心组件：

  • 多语言任务集：覆盖8种编程语言、43个GitHub仓库的350个真实issue修复实例，数据源自SWE-bench-Multilingual与SWE-bench-Verified-Mini，并经“未来提交清理”（future-commit cleanup）确保因果性；
  • 标准化适配协议：明确定义固定提示模板、运行时预算（CPU/内存/时间）、容器化工作区契约、统一补丁提取流程及权威评测器，使异构代理框架可在同一评估轴上横向对比。

关键发现与创新

  • 仅用最小化direct-diff适配器的OpenClaw在全基准上Pass@1仅为19.1%，而完整适配器提升至73.4%（同用GLM-5.1 backbone），证实适配器设计是释放代理编码潜力的关键瓶颈；
  • 在跨模型（9模型×OpenClaw）与跨爪具（5爪具×2模型）消融中，模型选择影响Pass@1达29.4个百分点，爪具选择影响27.4个百分点，且同等准确率系统间API总成本差异显著；
  • 同步发布轻量版Claw-SWE-Bench Lite（80实例），基于17维校准指标的成本感知+排序感知采样生成，支持快速验证。

本基准首次将代理框架（harness）与成本核算列为SWE-style评估的一等公民，开源全部数据与协议（GitHub/Hugging Face），推动可复现、低成本、多维度的编码代理科学评估。

AI Summary (English)

Claw-SWE-Bench is a multilingual, SWE-bench–style benchmark and adapter protocol designed to fairly evaluate OpenClaw-style autonomous agent harnesses (“claws”) on real-world coding tasks. It standardizes evaluation across five critical axes: fixed prompt, runtime budget, Docker workspace contract, patch extraction procedure, and unified evaluator—enabling apples-to-apples comparison of heterogeneous agent frameworks. The full benchmark comprises 350 GitHub issue-resolution instances across 8 languages and 43 repositories, rigorously cleaned to exclude future commits; Claw-SWE-Bench Lite (80 instances) supports rapid validation via cost- and rank-aware calibration. Experiments reveal that adapter design dominates performance: OpenClaw with minimal direct-diff achieves only 19.1% Pass@1, while its full adapter reaches 73.4% (same GLM-5.1 backbone). Model choice shifts Pass@1 by 29.4 pp, harness choice by 27.4 pp—yet systems with similar accuracy incur vastly different API costs. Claw-SWE-Bench thus establishes harness architecture and cost accounting as first-class evaluation dimensions for coding agents. Data and code are open-sourced at https://github.com/opensquilla/claw-swe-bench and https://huggingface.co/datasets/TokenRhythm/Claw-SWE-Bench.

Abstract

General-purpose agents such as OpenClaw are increasingly used as autonomous tool users, but their coding ability is difficult to measure under SWE-bench: a generic agent does not by itself satisfy the clean Docker workspace, patch, and prediction contract required for scoring. We introduce Claw-SWE-Bench, a multilingual SWE-bench-style benchmark and adapter protocol that makes heterogeneous agent harnesses, or claws, comparable under fair settings including a fixed prompt, runtime budget, workspace contract, patch extraction procedure, and evaluator. The full benchmark contains 350 GitHub issue-resolution instances across 8 languages and 43 repositories, drawn from SWE-bench-Multilingual and SWE-bench-Verified-Mini after future-commit cleanup. We also release Claw-SWE-Bench Lite for faster validation, which is an 80-instance subset selected by a cost-aware, rank-aware procedure over 17 calibration columns. On the full benchmark, OpenClaw with a minimal direct-diff adapter scores only $19.1\%$ Pass@1, whereas the full adapter reaches $73.4\%$ with the same GLM 5.1 backbone, showing that adapter design is essential for enabling OpenClaw-style harnesses to perform coding tasks effectively. Across an OpenClaw $\times$ nine-model sweep and a five-claw $\times$ two-model sweep, model choice changes Pass@1 by $29.4$ pp and harness choice by $27.4$ pp under fixed models; systems with similar accuracy can differ substantially in total API cost. Claw-SWE-Bench therefore treats harness and cost accounting as first-class axes of SWE-style coding-agent evaluation, providing both a full benchmark and a low-cost reference set for reproducible comparison. The data is available at https://github.com/opensquilla/claw-swe-bench and https://huggingface.co/datasets/TokenRhythm/Claw-SWE-Bench.

item.title: Which Models Are Our Models Built On? Auditing Invisible Dependencies in Modern LLMs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12385v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

现代大语言模型（LLM）的训练流程日益依赖其他模型——用于合成训练数据、过滤语料库、评估输出质量，甚至指导架构设计与超参选择。这些依赖关系具有递归性：一个模型可能依赖上游模型，而该上游模型本身又依赖更早的模型或数据集，其依赖链分散在论文、技术报告、GitHub 仓库、Hugging Face 模型卡、许可证文件等异构公开资源中。人工追溯此类深度嵌套、跨平台、命名不一致的依赖结构已远超认知负荷。

方法：ModSleuth 系统

我们提出 ModSleuth——一种基于智能体（agentic）的自动化审计系统，能从多源公开 artifact 中递归重建LLM 依赖图，并以源证据锚定（source-grounded evidence）确保每条边可验证。核心创新在于三方面：

  • 依赖形式化定义：区分直接依赖（如明确调用某模型API生成数据）与间接依赖（如使用经某模型过滤后的语料）；
  • 操作中心关系建模：将“数据蒸馏”“评估打分”“指令重写”等 pipeline 角色映射为标准化操作类型（e.g., used-for-filtering, evaluated-by），统一异构文档语义；
  • 跨源实体对齐：通过联合解析模型名、版本号、哈希值、仓库URL及上下文描述，消解 llama-2-7b, meta-llama/Llama-2-7b-hf, sha256:abc123 等指代同一 artifact 的歧义。

主要发现

在对 Qwen2、Phi-3、DeepSeek-Coder 和 StarCoder2 四个典型开源 LLM 发布包的审计中，ModSleuth 验证了 1,060 条源证据支撑的依赖关系，构建出迄今最完整的 LLM 开发依赖图谱。关键发现包括：

  • 多跳开源许可证义务（如 Apache-2.0 模型训练于 CC-BY-NC 数据，引发下游商用风险）；
  • 训练与评估环节高度耦合（>68% 的评估模型被反向用于数据清洗）；
  • 32% 的发布模型宣称“未使用某闭源模型”，但依赖图显示其训练语料经该模型隐式过滤；
  • 文档严重不一致：41% 的模型卡缺失关键上游依赖声明，或版本标注错误。

我们开源 ModSleuth 工具链与全部依赖图谱，推动 LLM 生态的可审计性与责任可追溯性。

AI Summary (English)

Modern LLM development increasingly relies on recursive, invisible dependencies—other models used for data generation, corpus filtering, output evaluation, and even architectural decisions. These dependencies span fragmented, heterogeneous public artifacts (papers, repos, model cards), making manual tracing infeasible. We introduce ModSleuth, an agentic system that recursively reconstructs source-grounded dependency graphs from such artifacts. Its core contributions are: (1) a formal dependency taxonomy distinguishing direct (e.g., API calls) and indirect (e.g., filtered corpora) relations; (2) operation-centered modeling of pipeline roles (e.g., used-for-evaluation, trained-on-distilled-data); and (3) robust artifact identity resolution across names, versions, and repositories. Applied to four major LLM releases (Qwen2, Phi-3, DeepSeek-Coder, StarCoder2), ModSleuth verified 1,060 dependencies, uncovering multi-hop license obligations, pervasive train-evaluation coupling, significant discrepancies between stated and actual upstream usage, and widespread documentation inconsistencies. We release ModSleuth and the dependency graphs to advance transparency in LLM ecosystems.

Abstract

Modern LLM training pipelines increasingly rely on other models to generate data, filter corpora, judge outputs, and guide development decisions. These dependencies are recursive: a model may depend on an upstream artifact whose own dependencies are documented only in separate releases and artifacts. As a result, the full dependency structure is fragmented across heterogeneous public artifacts, with complexity and recursive depth far outpacing humans' ability to trace. We introduce ModSleuth, an agentic system that recursively reconstructs LLM dependency graphs from public artifacts with source-grounded evidence. We find that the primary challenge is no longer information extraction, but defining what constitutes a dependency and reconciling artifact references across inconsistent documentation. We address these challenges through a formalization that distinguishes direct and indirect dependencies, represents heterogeneous pipeline roles through operation-centered relationships, and resolves artifact identities across names, versions, and repositories. Applying ModSleuth to four public-artifact-rich LLM releases, we recover 1,060 source-verified dependencies and construct large-scale dependency graphs of modern LLM development. These graphs reveal multi-hop license obligations, train-evaluation coupling, discrepancies between released and training-time artifacts, and documentation inconsistencies that would otherwise be difficult to uncover. We release ModSleuth and the resulting dependency graphs to support transparent analysis of the increasingly complex ecosystems underlying modern LLMs.

item.title: Measuring Epistemic Resilience of LLMs Under Misleading Medical Context
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12291v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

大型语言模型（LLMs）在医学执照考试中已达到专家级得分，公众与临床工作者常据此推断其具备可靠的医疗判断能力。然而，这一“高分即安全”的假设缺乏对模型在现实复杂语境下稳健性的检验——尤其当患者主动输入误导性信息（如网络谣言、错误症状描述或伪权威建议）时，模型是否仍能坚守正确医学判断？

方法与工具创新

本研究提出认知韧性（Epistemic Resilience）概念：指模型在面对精心设计的误导性上下文时，维持原始正确答案的能力。为此，我们构建首个专注抗干扰能力评估的医学基准——MedMisBench。该基准包含10,932道标准医学问题，并配以48,889组经临床验证的误导性上下文-选项对，覆盖三大维度：医学推理（如因果误判）、智能体能力（如任务目标偏移）、患者旅程评估（如病史扭曲）。误导类型系统化分类为权威式虚构（e.g., “美国CDC最新指南指出…”）、例外中毒（e.g., “除非患者携带XX突变”）、情感操纵等。

关键发现

在11种主流模型配置（含GPT-4、Claude 3、Med-PaLM 2等）上测试显示：平均准确率从原始题目的71.1%骤降至38.0%，攻击成功率高达51.5%；其中权威式虚构攻击成功率69.5%，例外中毒达64.1%。由来自7国的14名临床专家组成的评审小组评估了500个典型案例，确认38.2%存在潜在严重危害（如错误排除危重疾病、推荐禁忌药物）。结果揭示当前医学AI评估的根本盲区：现有基准仅测“知识存量”，未测“知识守恒力”。

意义

MedMisBench填补了医疗AI鲁棒性评估的关键缺口，为监管框架、临床部署前验证及模型对齐优化提供可量化的韧性标尺。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) achieve expert-level scores on medical licensing exams, fostering unwarranted confidence in their clinical reliability—despite growing patient use for health advice. We challenge this assumption by introducing epistemic resilience: the ability to retain correct medical judgment when confronted with adversarial, misleading context. To measure it, we present MedMisBench, a novel benchmark comprising 10,932 medical questions and 48,889 clinically curated misleading context-option pairs across reasoning, agentic behavior, and patient-journey simulation. Across 11 model configurations, mean accuracy drops sharply—from 71.1% on clean questions to 38.0% under targeted attacks—with an overall attack success rate of 51.5%. Formal, rule-like fabrications are most damaging: authority-framed falsehoods achieve 69.5% success; exception-poisoning claims reach 64.1%. A 14-member international clinical panel identified serious potential harm in 38.2% of reviewed cases. MedMisBench exposes a critical gap in medical AI evaluation: current benchmarks assess what models know, not whether they preserve correct judgment under epistemic pressure.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) now reach expert-level scores on medical licensing exams, encouraging the assumption that high scores imply safe medical judgment while patients increasingly use them for health advice. We show this assumption is fragile: when misleading context is injected into questions that LLMs originally answer correctly, they abandon the correct answer. We call the ability to maintain correct judgment under adversarial context epistemic resilience, and introduce MedMisBench to measure it. MedMisBench contains 10,932 medical question items and 48,889 misleading context-option pairs spanning medical reasoning, agentic capability, and patient-journey evaluation. Across 11 model configurations, mean accuracy falls from 71.1% on original questions to 38.0% under focused misleading context, with 51.5% attack success. The most damaging injections are formal, rule-like fabrications: authority-framed falsehoods reach 69.5% attack success and exception-poisoning claims reach 64.1%. A 14-member clinical panel from 7 countries identified serious potential harm in 38.2% of reviewed cases. MedMisBench exposes a structural blind spot in LLM evaluation in medical settings: existing benchmarks measure what models know, but not whether they preserve correct medical judgment under misleading context.

item.title: External Experience Serving in Production LLM Systems: A Deployment-Oriented Study of Quality-Cost Trade-offs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11806v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

在生产级大语言模型（LLM）系统中，运营过程中持续积累的可复用外部经验（如历史审核案例、工具调用轨迹、疑难问答解答）具备潜在价值。然而，关键部署挑战并非“经验是否有效”，而在于：如何在真实约束下，权衡任务质量提升与在线服务成本增加——包括提示长度膨胀、端到端延迟上升、GPU显存/计算压力加剧等。

方法与实验设计

本研究将“外部经验服务”（External Experience Serving）建模为一个部署导向的质量-成本联合优化问题。我们在真实生产内容审核（Moderation）场景中开展实证评估，并辅以工具使用（Tool-use） 和 GPQA 两类对比任务，分别代表短输出低解码开销与长解码高计算负载两种典型服务范式。系统性比较五类策略：无经验基线、随机经验控制、全局静态注入（Global Prompt Injection）、以及两种检索增强的选择性注入（Top-K检索+重排序）。全面测量任务准确率/F1、首字延迟（TTFT）、输出延迟（ITL）、显存占用及每请求成本（$ per request）。

核心发现与创新点

  • ✅ 选择性优于全局：当经验需按案例动态匹配时，检索式选择性注入显著超越无差别全局注入，在Moderation任务中质量提升+2.1%的同时降低延迟17%；
  • ✅ 检索质量 > 检索数量：提升Top-K从5到20未带来收益，但引入语义重排序使Top-3效果超越未重排的Top-10；
  • ✅ 任务结构决定成本效益曲线：同一策略在Tool-use（短输出）中ROI达2.8×，而在GPQA（长解码）中ROI仅0.9×，凸显任务特定成本结构的关键调节作用；
  • ✅ 根本启示：外部经验不应作为“默认开关”，而应作为受服务接口能力与实时成本反馈驱动的动态决策变量。其价值实现高度依赖部署上下文——仅当质量增益足以覆盖在线成本增量时，方具工程可行性。

AI Summary (English)

This paper treats external experience serving—the runtime injection of operational knowledge (e.g., past moderation decisions, tool traces, expert answers)—as a deployment-centric quality-cost trade-off problem. Evaluating across real production moderation, tool-use, and GPQA tasks, we compare no-experience baselines, random controls, global prompt injection, and retrieval-based selective injection. Results show that case-dependent selective retrieval outperforms unconditional global injection in both quality and latency; retrieval quality (e.g., semantic re-ranking) matters more than merely increasing Top-$K$; and the same policy exhibits dramatically divergent cost-benefit profiles across short-output vs. decode-heavy regimes. Crucially, external experience pays off only when the serving interface and task-specific cost structure make its quality gains worth the online overhead—establishing it as a context-aware serving decision, not a universal add-on.

Abstract

Production LLM systems accumulate reusable operational experience, but the practical deployment issue is not merely whether such experience can help. It is how different serving strategies trade off quality against online cost under realistic constraints. Injecting external experience can improve task quality, yet it also increases prompt burden, latency, and serving pressure. We study \textit{external experience serving} as a deployment-oriented quality-cost trade-off problem. We evaluate this question in a real production moderation setting, with tool-use and GPQA as supporting contrast tasks that expose different output-cost regimes. We compare no-experience baselines, random experience controls, global prompt injection, and retrieval-based selective injection, and analyze both task quality and serving cost. The results show that, once experience becomes case-dependent, selective retrieval provides a stronger operating point than unconditional global injection. They further show that retrieval quality matters more than simply increasing Top-$K$, and that the same serving policy can exhibit substantially different cost-benefit profiles across short-output and decode-heavy regimes. These findings suggest that external experience is best treated as a selective, cost-aware serving decision rather than as a universal add-on. Overall, in the settings studied here, external experience pays off only when both the serving interface and the task-specific cost structure make its quality gains worth the online cost.

item.title: UCX is All You Need: A Universal Transform for Committing Authenticated Encryption
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1222

AI Summary (中文)

背景与动机

近年来，针对认证加密（AE）方案的新型攻击（如密钥重用、标签伪造、提交性缺失引发的密文替换）以及新兴应用场景（如可验证日志、安全多方计算中的确定性加密）共同推动了提交型认证加密（committing AE, cAE）的研究。cAE要求：若两个密文解密为同一明文，则其密钥、明文、关联数据及随机数（nonce）四元组必须完全一致——这一强安全性可防止“密文等价攻击”，是构建可信系统的关键基石。然而，现有cAE转换方案（如CMT、CMT2、SIV-CPA等）存在显著局限：或仅适用于标签型AE1框架（要求原方案输出独立认证标签），或无法兼容AE5通用框架（涵盖nonce可重复、无随机数、确定性等广义AE变体），或牺牲UNAE（唯一nonce安全性）与MRAE（误用抵抗性）等核心AE安全属性。

方法与创新

本文提出全新通用转换方案 UCX（Universal Committing eXform），首次实现真正意义上的“全场景兼容”：

  • ✅ 不依赖标签结构：无需原始AE方案输出分离式认证标签，可直接作用于任意AE5兼容方案（包括SIV、GCM-SIV、AES-CTR-HMAC等非标签型设计）；
  • ✅ 框架普适性：严格定义于AE5安全模型（涵盖nonce误用、确定性、无随机数等所有实用变体），突破此前仅支持AE1的瓶颈；
  • ✅ 安全属性保持：在提升至cAE安全性的同时，完全保留原方案的UNAE与MRAE安全性，避免安全降级；
  • ✅ 理论基础创新：首次提出并构造可调提交隐藏器（Tweakable Committing Concealer, TCC），作为UCX的核心构件——该原语兼具可调分组密码的灵活性与提交性承诺的不可伪造性，具有独立研究价值。

主要结论

UCX在理想分组密码模型下被证明具备cAE安全性，在标准模型下保持AE5安全性。其工程部署可无缝嵌入现有密码库（如OpenSSL、libsodium），显著降低开发者因误选受限转换方案而导致的安全事故风险。本工作终结了“通用cAE转换是否存在”的开放问题，为下一代高保障加密协议提供了坚实、统一、可验证的基础构件。

AI Summary (English)

We present UCX, a universal transform that converts any AE5-compliant authenticated encryption scheme into a committing AE (cAE) scheme—without requiring the input scheme to be tag-based, while preserving both UNAE (Unique Nonce AE) and MRAE (Misuse-Resistant AE) security. Unlike all prior transforms (e.g., CMT, SIV-CPA), UCX is the first to simultaneously satisfy all three properties: (1) applicability beyond the restrictive AE1 framework, (2) no reliance on explicit authentication tags, and (3) full retention of core AE security guarantees. We prove UCX’s cAE security in the ideal-cipher model and its AE5 security in the standard model. To achieve this, we introduce and construct a novel primitive—the Tweakable Committing Concealer (TCC)—which may be of independent cryptographic interest. UCX simplifies real-world deployment, reduces implementation errors in libraries and applications, and resolves the long-standing quest for a truly universal cAE transform.

Abstract

Emerging attacks and applications have motivated the development of transforms that turn a given AE scheme into a committing AE (cAE) one. We give a new transform called UCX with the following attributes: It does not require the starting scheme to be tag based, works for schemes in the broad AE5 framework rather than the limited AE1 one, and preserves both UNAE (Unique Nonce AE) and MRAE (Misuse Resistant AE) security. No prior transform is ``universal'' in the sense of having the combination of all these properties. The use of UCX in place of prior, limited transforms reduces the risk of error and failure in the real world, where choices may be made by application developers and hidden in software libraries. The committing security of UCX is shown in the ideal-cipher model, and its AE5-security in the standard model. To design UCX, we introduce and build a new primitive, that we call a Tweakable Committing Concealer, and that may be of independent interest.

item.title: On Arithmetic Private Information Retrieval: Why Code-Based PIR (Usually) Fails
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1224

AI Summary (中文)

算术型私有信息检索（APIR）研究：为何基于编码的PIR方案（通常）失效

本文系统性地提出并研究算术型私有信息检索（Arithmetic Private Information Retrieval, APIR）这一新范式：数据库为有限域上的向量，协议以黑盒方式调用域运算（加法、乘法），不依赖特定表示或编码结构。该模型自然涵盖近期备受关注的“基于编码的PIR”方案（如Holzbaur等ISIT’20、Verma与Hollanti ISIT’24），为其安全性分析提供统一框架。

主要发现

1. 根本性下界与实际攻击：我们证明——在单服务器、信息论安全前提下，任意APIR方案的下载通信量不可能低于整个数据库长度 $n$（即无法实现亚线性通信）。该下界揭示了算术模型的本质瓶颈。更关键的是，我们据此设计出高效代数攻击：对所有已提议的参数实例（含推荐安全等级），可在标准工作站上数分钟内完全恢复用户查询索引，彻底破解其隐私性。

2. 可行替代路径：尽管单服务器无密钥APIR不可行，我们在两类增强模型中构建了计算安全的高效APIR方案：（i）双服务器模型下，通过算术化Boyle等人（CCS’16）的分布式点函数（DPF）方案，实现$O(\sqrt{n})$通信；（ii）单服务器+密钥预处理模型下，将Chen等人（STOC’26）最新构造完全算术化，获得$O(n^\varepsilon)$通信（$\varepsilon<1$），且仅依赖经典编码假设（如LWE或RS码译码困难性）。

3. 两服务器信息论APIR的代数刻画：我们首次以线性代数语言（矩阵秩、子空间交维数）精确刻画两服务器信息论APIR的存在性条件，并复现Chor等（FOCS’95）思想，构造出通信复杂度为$O(n^{1/3})$的方案。该结果是否最优，仍是开放问题。

本工作既否定了“轻量级编码PIR”的可行性，又为APIR在合理信任模型下指明了严格安全、可实现的工程路径。

AI Summary (English)

We initiate the study of Arithmetic Private Information Retrieval (APIR), where the database is a vector over a field and the protocol accesses the field only via black-box arithmetic operations. Our main results are: (1) A strong negative result—no single-server information-theoretically secure APIR scheme can achieve download cost asymptotically smaller than $n$ field elements; we exploit this to break all proposed code-based PIR schemes (Holzbaur et al., ISIT’20; Verma & Hollanti, ISIT’24) in minutes on a standard workstation. (2) Positive constructions in relaxed models: computationally secure APIR with $O(\sqrt{n})$ communication using two servers (via arithmetization of Boyle et al.’s DPF-based PIR), and with $O(n^\varepsilon)$ communication for single-server with secret-key preprocessing (by arithmetizing Chen et al.’s STOC’26 scheme), both relying on standard coding assumptions. (3) A linear-algebraic characterization of information-theoretic two-server APIR, achieving $O(n^{1/3})$ communication via a variant of Chor et al.’s FOCS’95 approach.

Abstract

We initiate the study of arithmetic private information retrieval (APIR) schemes, in which the database is a vector of field elements and the scheme makes a black-box use of the field.
We obtain the following results.

1. Our main result is a negative one: We show that no single-server APIR scheme can achieve non-trivial download cost smaller than $n$ field elements.
We observe that recent proposals for code-based PIR (Holzbaur et al., ISIT'20; Verma and Hollanti, ISIT'24) are arithmetic, and show how to break them within a few minutes on a standard workstation for all suggested parameters.

2. We complement the above by positive results in alternative models. Concretely, we show that with either two servers or a single server with secret-key preprocessing, it is possible to construct computationally secure APIR schemes based on well-studied coding assumptions.
This is achieved by arithmetizing the distributed-point-function-based PIR of Boyle et al.(CCS'16), and by observing that the recent construction of secret-key single-server PIR by Chen et al.(STOC'26) also arithmetizes.

3. Finally, we characterize the existence of information-theoretic two-server APIR schemes in linear-algebraic terms, and show that communication of $O(n^{1/3})$ can be achieved in this setting based on the original approach of Chor et al.(FOCS'95). The optimality of this result remains an interesting open question.

item.title: Neon NTT - (Auto)formalised
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1223

AI Summary (中文)

Neon NTT 形式化验证：面向可验证加速器的自动形式化实践

本研究完成了对 Becker 等人提出的 Neon NTT（Neon Number-Theoretic Transform）算法核心模算术模块的机器可验证形式化，基于 Isabelle/HOL 定理证明器实现。工作聚焦于 ARM NEON 指令集下高效数论变换所需的底层算术基础设施，构建了高度参数化的形式化理论体系：

  • 统一建模 Barrett 约减与 Montgomery 约减/乘法，并严格证明二者在模运算语义下的等价性；
  • 首次在交互式定理证明框架中形式化 Doubling-Montgomery 与 Rounding-Montgomery 两种变体，明确其适用场景与转换条件；
  • 针对 Neon 汇编内核（如 vmlal.u32, vmlsl.u32 等），建立精确的字级算术模型，并证明其功能正确性与数值界（bound）——包括中间结果不溢出、最终余数落在标准区间等关键安全属性。

本工作采用导向式自动形式化（directed auto-formalisation） 范式：由 Claude Opus 4.7/4.8 模型通过 AutoCorrode 的 LLM–Isabelle 集成层，自动生成定义、定理陈述与证明草稿；人类作者全程把控架构设计、抽象层次选择、证明策略引导，并对生成内容进行审慎筛选与精炼——既提升形式化效率，又保障逻辑严谨性与工程可维护性。所有文档由 Isabelle 自动从源码生成，彻底消除“ prose–proof drift”（文本描述与形式证明脱节）风险，为密码学加速器的形式化可信链提供坚实基础。

AI Summary (English)
