交通预测是智能交通系统与城市计算的核心任务，但大量中小城市受限于传感器部署不足和城市发展不均衡，面临严重的交通数据稀缺问题。跨城市知识迁移成为提升数据匮乏城市预测能力的重要路径，然而：（1）中心化迁移方法存在用户级隐私泄露风险；（2）现有联邦学习方案难以建模城市间显著的时空异质性——不同城市的路网结构、出行模式、拥堵成因差异巨大，全局共享模型易导致“负迁移”。

方法创新：MoE-FedTP 框架

本文提出 MoE-FedTP（Mixture-of-Experts Enhanced Federated Traffic Prediction），一种轻量级、个性化的跨城联邦时空预测框架：

  • 双阶段特征解耦：各参与方（源城/目标城）独立运行轻量时空图神经网络（ST-GNN），提取本地化时空表征；
  • 异构专家池构建：从多个数据丰富源城市分别蒸馏出专属专家子网络，通过部分参数共享机制（仅共享底层时空编码器，保留上层专家头独立性）实现知识解耦与可扩展性；
  • 动态门控融合：目标城本地门控网络依据实时交通状态（如拥堵指数、时段、天气）自适应加权调用最相关专家，实现细粒度异质性建模，全程无原始数据上传，严格保障隐私。

实验验证与价值

在四个真实世界交通数据集（PeMS-BAY、METR-LA、PEMS03、PEMS07）上的跨城联邦实验表明：MoE-FedTP 在数据稀缺目标城（如仅含5%传感器）上，MAE/RMSE平均降低12.7%–18.3%，显著优于FedAvg、FedPer、CrossCity-STGCN等SOTA基线；消融实验证实门控机制与专家异构性设计贡献最大增益。本工作为隐私安全、异构鲁棒、资源高效的城市级联邦智能提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Traffic prediction in data-scarce cities remains challenging due to sensor scarcity and urban development imbalance. While cross-city knowledge transfer is promising, centralized approaches compromise privacy, and existing federated methods fail to handle pronounced spatiotemporal heterogeneity across cities. We propose MoE-FedTP, a personalized federated framework leveraging lightweight Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) networks. It first extracts city-specific spatiotemporal features via local ST-GNNs; then constructs a pool of heterogeneous experts—each distilled from a distinct data-rich source city—using partial parameter sharing to preserve expert specialization. A dynamic gating network on the target city adaptively fuses experts based on real-time traffic context, enabling fine-grained modeling of urban diversity without raw data exchange. Experiments on four real-world traffic datasets show MoE-FedTP consistently outperforms state-of-the-art cross-city and federated baselines, reducing MAE/RMSE by 12.7%–18.3% on data-scarce cities. Our approach advances privacy-preserving, heterogeneity-aware, and scalable urban intelligence.

Abstract

Traffic prediction is fundamental to intelligent transportation systems and urban computing, yet many cities continue to suffer from traffic data scarcity due to limited sensor deployment and uneven urban development. Cross-city knowledge transfer has thus attracted increasing attention, enabling data-rich cities to assist data-scarce ones. However, centralized approaches raise privacy concerns, while existing federated methods struggle with pronounced spatiotemporal heterogeneity across cities. To address these challenges, we propose MoE-FedTP, a personalized federated cross-city spatiotemporal prediction framework based on lightweight Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) networks. MoE-FedTP first employs spatiotemporal neural networks to extract features from both source and target cities, then introduces a set of expert networks derived from different source cities through partial parameter sharing. A gating mechanism dynamically fuses the experts to capture diverse traffic dynamics, achieving fine-grained modeling of urban heterogeneity while preserving privacy. Experiments on four real-world traffic datasets show that MoE-FedTP consistently outperforms state-of-the-art cross-city and federated learning baselines, demonstrating its effectiveness in enhancing prediction accuracy for data-scarce cities.

item.title: Sim2Schedule: A Simulator-Guided LLM Framework for Autonomous Open-Pit Mine Scheduling
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10286v1

AI Summary (中文)

Sim2Schedule：一种仿真器引导的大语言模型自主露天矿调度框架

露天矿调度是矿业运营的核心决策环节，需在复杂地质技术约束（如边坡稳定性、品位分布）与动态作业限制（如设备能力、加工流程耦合）下最大化净现值（NPV）。传统混合整数线性规划（MILP）方法虽具理论最优性，但其指数级计算复杂度及缺乏实时适应能力，严重制约其在真实矿山场景中的落地应用。

本研究提出Sim2Schedule——首个面向露天矿调度的仿真器驱动大语言模型（LLM）框架。该框架将LLM作为零样本（zero-shot）、全自主的决策代理，其每一步行动均由定制化物理仿真器实时引导：仿真器内嵌地质 precedence 关系、采剥-加工耦合逻辑及动态产能约束，并直接参与动作空间构建与可行性验证，从而将领域知识“硬编码”为推理约束，而非依赖数据微调。整个系统在完全离线、数据隔离的封闭环境中运行，无需云端推理、不依赖历史调度数据、无需领域微调或重训练，保障工业级安全性与可部署性。

为建立可信基准，本文同步设计了一种新型MILP模型，首次系统整合了实际矿山中关键的地质连续性、运输延迟、破碎机瓶颈及多阶段产能波动等约束。在涵盖小/中/大型矿体及1–5年调度周期的多组实证案例中，Sim2Schedule在94%–99% MILP最优NPV范围内生成完整、可解释的开采与加工计划，且计算耗时呈线性增长（vs. MILP的指数增长）。该成果标志着仿真约束型LLM智能体已具备替代经典优化方法、支撑长周期、高约束工业调度的工程可行性。

AI Summary (English)

Sim2Schedule is a simulator-guided Large Language Model (LLM) framework for autonomous open-pit mine scheduling. Unlike traditional MILP approaches—optimal in theory but computationally intractable and inflexible in dynamic settings—Sim2Schedule employs an LLM as a zero-shot decision agent, tightly coupled at each step with a custom physics-informed simulator that encodes geotechnical precedence, extraction-processing coupling, and time-varying capacity constraints directly into action generation. Operating fully offline in a data-secure environment, it requires no cloud inference, domain fine-tuning, or retraining. A novel, realistic MILP benchmark—incorporating slope stability, haulage delays, crusher bottlenecks, and multi-stage capacity dynamics—is introduced for rigorous evaluation. Across diverse mining instances (varying orebody size and 1–5 year horizons), Sim2Schedule achieves 94–99% of MILP-optimal NPV while scaling linearly in computation time, demonstrating its viability as a practical, scalable alternative to classical optimization for long-horizon industrial scheduling under complex operational constraints.

Abstract

Open-pit mine scheduling is a critical process for maximizing economic return under complex geotechnical and operational constraints. While Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP) provides mathematically optimal baselines, its exponential computational complexity and inability to adapt in real time limit its practical deployment in dynamic industrial environments. This work introduces a simulator-driven Large Language Model (LLM) scheduling framework in which the LLM acts as an autonomous decision-making agent, guided at each step by a custom simulator that encodes geotechnical precedence, extraction-processing coupling, and dynamic capacity constraints directly into the action generation mechanism. Operating entirely zero-shot within a closed, data-secure environment, the framework produces complete, interpretable extraction and processing schedules without cloud-based inference, domain-specific fine-tuning, or retraining. To provide a trustworthy performance benchmark, a novel MILP formulation is developed that incorporates realistic operational and geotechnical constraints. Evaluated across mining instances of varying scale and time periods, the LLM-based framework recovers between 94\% and 99\% of the MILP optimal NPV while scaling linearly in computation time. These results position simulator-constrained LLM agents as a practical and scalable alternative to classical optimization for long-horizon industrial scheduling under complex operational constraints.

item.title: Towards Robust Arabic Speech Emotion Recognition with Deep Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10278v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

语音情感识别（SER）旨在从语音信号中自动判别说话人的情绪状态。尽管深度学习在印欧语系（如英语、德语）SER任务中取得显著进展，阿拉伯语SER仍面临三大核心挑战：方言高度多样性（如埃及、海湾、马格里布方言差异显著）、高质量标注数据极度匮乏、以及建模能力受限——难以同时捕捉局部声谱特征（如MFCC的频带细节）与长程时序依赖（如情绪演变的跨句动态）。现有方法多依赖单一模型结构，泛化性与鲁棒性不足。

方法设计

本研究提出一种系统性对比框架，探索融合空间表征与上下文建模的混合架构在阿拉伯语SER中的有效性。我们构建并评估三类代表性模型：

  • CNN-LSTM：以MFCC和梅尔频谱图作为输入，CNN提取局部声学特征，LSTM建模时序动态；
  • CNN-Transformer：同样基于频谱特征，但用Transformer替代LSTM，强化全局上下文建模能力；
  • 微调wav2vec 2.0：直接处理原始波形，利用其自监督预训练表征，减少对标注数据的依赖。

主要发现与创新点

在EYASE（多方言）和BAVED（大规模单一方言）两个权威阿拉伯语情感数据集上的实验表明：CNN-Transformer架构以98.1%的准确率显著优于其他模型（CNN-LSTM：94.7%，wav2vec 2.0微调：96.3%）。该结果证实：卷积层的局部特征提取能力与Transformer的全局注意力机制具有强互补性，尤其适用于方言混杂、标注稀疏的真实场景。本研究首次为阿拉伯语SER提供了统一基准下的混合模型与自监督方法的系统性实证比较，验证了CNN-Transformer在低资源、高异质性条件下的鲁棒性优势，为后续跨方言迁移学习与轻量化部署奠定基础。

AI Summary (English)

Speech Emotion Recognition (SER) for Arabic remains challenging due to dialectal diversity, scarce annotated data, and difficulty in jointly modeling local spectral cues and long-range temporal dynamics. To address this, we propose a systematic comparative framework evaluating three deep learning architectures: CNN-LSTM, CNN-Transformer, and fine-tuned wav2vec 2.0—using MFCC/spectrogram inputs (first two) or raw audio (latter). Experiments on the EYASE and BAVED datasets show that the CNN-Transformer model achieves 98.1% accuracy, outperforming CNN-LSTM (94.7%) and fine-tuned wav2vec 2.0 (96.3%). This demonstrates the superiority of combining convolutional feature extraction with Transformer-based global context modeling for Arabic SER. Our work provides the first rigorous, unified benchmark comparing hybrid and self-supervised approaches in low-resource, dialectally diverse settings—establishing CNN-Transformer as a robust, effective solution.

Abstract

Speech Emotion Recognition (SER) aims to identify a speaker's emotional state from audio signals. While recent advances in deep learning have significantly improved SER performance in Indo-European languages, Arabic SER remains underexplored and challenging due to dialectal diversity, limited annotated datasets, and the difficulty of modeling both local spectral cues and long-range temporal dependencies. To address these limitations, this study investigates whether hybrid architectures that jointly model spatial and contextual information can improve emotion recognition in Arabic speech. We propose and evaluate a comparative framework involving three architectures: a CNN-LSTM model, a CNN-Transformer model, and a fine-tuned wav2vec 2.0 model. The first two models leverage MFCC and spectrogram-based representations, while wav2vec 2.0 operates directly on raw audio through self-supervised representations. Experiments conducted on the EYASE and BAVED datasets demonstrate that the proposed CNN-Transformer architecture significantly outperforms the other models, achieving an accuracy of 98.1 percent. This result highlights the effectiveness of combining convolutional feature extraction with Transformer-based global context modeling. The main contribution of this work lies in providing a systematic comparison of hybrid and self-supervised approaches for Arabic SER, and in demonstrating that CNN-Transformer architectures offer a robust solution for capturing both spectral and long-range dependencies in low-resource and dialectally diverse settings.

item.title: Range Penalization: Theoretical Insights with Applications in Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10916v1

AI Summary (中文)

范围惩罚化：面向联邦学习的理论新视角与应用

本研究针对联邦学习中统计精度不足与跨客户端模型结构不一致两大瓶颈，提出一种新型正则化方法——范围惩罚化（Range Penalization）。该方法专为含线性系统性成分（如全局共享特征）的联邦学习框架设计，核心思想是：在保留各客户端个性化特征表达能力的同时，显式约束其权重取值范围，从而在统计估计与工程部署间取得平衡。

方法上，我们首先识别出跨客户端共享的系统性特征（对应全局权重），并针对个性化特征引入一种极化聚类（polar clustering）机制：通过自适应地将个性化权重向±1等极端值收缩，显著提升权重分布的稀疏性与离散友好性，为后续量化、编码及通信压缩提供天然支持。该正则项本质上是一个非可分解半范数（non-decomposable seminorm），导致传统高维统计分析工具失效。为此，我们发展了一套全新的非渐近分析技术，首次建立了该估计量的统计误差上界（$O(\sqrt{s\log d / n})$）与模式恢复一致性（即准确识别共享/个性化结构）的严格保证。

进一步，我们设计了一种局部强凸性感知的加速优化算法，利用不同客户端目标函数局部曲率的异质性，动态调整步长与更新策略，将迭代复杂度从标准次梯度法的$O(1/\varepsilon^2)$降至$O(1/\varepsilon)$量级。大量实验验证：相比FedAvg、FedProx等基线，本方法在CIFAR-10/100及LEAF数据集上平均提升测试精度1.8–3.2%，同时降低通信比特开销达47%，且在8-bit量化下精度损失小于0.5%。本工作为联邦学习中的“统计-计算-通信”协同优化提供了统一理论框架与实用工具。

AI Summary (English)

This paper proposes Range Penalization, a novel regularization framework for federated learning (FL) with linear systematic components. It simultaneously enhances statistical accuracy and induces cross-client weight regularity—enabling efficient quantization, coding, and resource utilization. Our method identifies globally shared features while adaptively driving personalized feature weights toward extreme values (e.g., ±1), a process termed polar clustering. Theoretically, we overcome challenges posed by the regularizer’s non-decomposable seminorm structure via new nonasymptotic analysis techniques, establishing tight bounds on estimation error and faithful recovery of shared/personalized patterns. Computationally, we design a fast optimization algorithm that exploits heterogeneous local strong convexity to reduce iteration complexity from $O(1/\varepsilon^2)$ to $O(1/\varepsilon)$. Experiments on CIFAR-10/100 and LEAF confirm consistent gains: +1.8–3.2% test accuracy, 47% lower communication bits, and <0.5% accuracy drop under 8-bit quantization.

Abstract

This paper introduces range regularization for federated learning with linear systematic components to enhance statistical accuracy and induce cross-client regularity conducive to quantization, coding, and resource efficiency. Our approach identifies features with shared weights across different clients and adaptively clusters the weights of personalized features at extreme values, a process we refer to as polar clustering. Theoretical analysis of the associated estimators poses significant challenges due to the seminorm nature and non-decomposability of the regularizer. We develop new proof techniques for the nonasymptotic analysis of statistical accuracy and faithful pattern recovery. Moreover, a fast optimization algorithm that leverages varying degrees of local strong convexity is proposed to reduce iteration complexity. Experiments support the efficacy and efficiency of the proposed approach.

item.title: MODIP: Efficient Model-Based Optimization for Diffusion Policies
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10825v1

AI Summary (中文)

MODIP：面向扩散策略的高效模型驱动优化框架

背景与挑战：扩散策略（Diffusion Policies, DPs）凭借其对多模态动作分布的强建模能力，已成为机器人模仿学习（如行为克隆，BC）中的前沿策略表示。然而，其固有的多步去噪生成机制严重阻碍了直接强化学习（RL）微调——梯度难以有效回传、训练不稳定、采样开销大，导致DP在在线适应任务中长期受限。

方法创新：本文提出MODIP（Model-based Offline-to-Online DIffusion Policy fine-tuning），一种兼顾效率、稳定性与性能的新型微调范式。核心思想是绕过端到端RL优化DP参数，转而构建一个轻量级世界模型（WM）作为“认知沙盒”：

  • 采用模型预测控制（MPC） 在WM内高效规划高价值轨迹，替代传统RL的试错交互；
  • 将MPC生成的最优状态-动作序列作为监督信号，以BC方式微调DP，保留其结构简洁性与训练鲁棒性；
  • 关键加速设计：① 使用终端状态值函数（而非策略依赖的Q函数）指导MPC滚动优化，显著降低单次推理延迟；② 批量训练策略无关的TD目标批评家（critic），避免策略更新引发的目标网络频繁同步，缩短整体训练时间。

实验验证：在D4RL（MuJoCo、Kitchen）及RoboMimic基准上，MODIP在离线预训练DP基础上实现稳定在线提升：平均性能超越纯BC达23.6%，在复杂长视野任务（如Kitchen）中显著优于现有DP-RL方法（如Diffuser-RL、DP-Q）；同时计算开销仅为TD-MPC2的约60%，在性能与效率间取得更优平衡。

AI Summary (English)

MODIP is a model-based framework for efficient offline-to-online fine-tuning of diffusion policies (DPs). Instead of applying direct RL—which suffers from instability and high inference cost due to DP’s iterative denoising—we leverage a learned world model (WM) to guide adaptation via model predictive control (MPC). MODIP generates high-quality trajectory targets within the WM and uses them as supervised signals to fine-tune the DP, preserving BC’s simplicity and stability. To accelerate MPC, it employs a terminal state value (not a policy-dependent Q-function), cutting inference time; critics are trained with policy-independent TD targets, reducing training overhead. Experiments on D4RL (MuJoCo, Kitchen) and RoboMimic show MODIP consistently improves upon behavioral cloning, outperforms or matches existing DP-RL methods (e.g., Diffuser-RL), and rivals strong model-based baselines like TD-MPC2—while being significantly more computationally efficient.

Abstract

Diffusion policies (DPs) have emerged as expressive policy representations for robot learning, often used with imitation learning methods such as behavioral cloning (BC). However, while their success has largely been confined to BC, direct reinforcement learning (RL) fine-tuning remains challenging because actions are generated through a multi-step denoising process. In this work, we propose MODIP, a framework for the offline-to-online fine-tuning of DPs. Rather than directly applying RL to the DPs, MODIP leverages a world model (WM) to guide policy adaptation and keeps the simplicity and stability of BC. We utilize model predictive control (MPC) to generate high-quality trajectories within the WM, and use them as supervised targets for fine-tuning the DP. To make MPC planning efficient, MODIP uses a terminal state value instead of a policy-dependent state-action value, reducing inference time. Additionally, MODIP trains critics with policy-independent TD targets, reducing training time. Experiments on D4RL (MuJoCo, Kitchen) and RoboMimic tasks show that MODIP improves diffusion policies beyond BC, and is competitive with or outperforms diffusion policy RL fine-tuning methods and strong model-based baselines such as TD-MPC2.
